## Phase 6/9 Follow-up: TodoMVC Scoped Edit Text Route Parity

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T21:18:12+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-edit-commit-root-list-scenario-run-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-edit-cancel-root-list-scenario-run-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-edit-blur-root-list-scenario-run-plan.json`

Implemented:

- Added internal `UpdateGuard` propagation for source-payload literal branches,
  including Enter/Escape key guards.
- Lowered guards into typed `MachinePlan` update branches as
  `PlanSourceGuard::SourcePayloadOneOf`.
- Added plan verifier coverage for source guard typed refs.
- Extended CPU PlanExecutor indexed updates for TodoMVC edit routes:
  `SourcePayload`, `Const`, `BoolNot`, `ReadPath`, `PreviousValue`, and
  `TextTrimOrPrevious`.
- Added per-event row-resolution caching so multiple indexed update branches
  for one source event keep the same row identity after an earlier branch changes
  the row title.
- Mirrored guarded indexed replay, per-event row-resolution caching, and changed
  diagnostics in `boon_report_schema`.
- Fixed legacy source-route guard handling:
  - source-action artifacts now preserve guards;
  - indexed text/bool actions skip guarded non-matches before executor side
    effects;
  - root scalar route targets also skip guarded non-matches;
  - scalar equation artifacts preserve `ScalarUpdateBranch.guard`.
- Removed a legacy duplicate semantic delta from TodoMVC Enter commit by making
  the old edit-title compatibility path a fallback only when generic source
  actions did not already emit the row `title` field.
- Kept `ReadPath` draft-reset semantics as an explicit semantic `FieldSet` even
  when the stored value is unchanged; the report row still records
  `changed=false`. This matches the human scenario expectation that opening edit
  mode resets `edit_text` from `title`.
- Added full per-step legacy and plan semantic delta payloads to
  `run-plan-root-scalar-scenario` comparison reports so collapsed signatures are
  no longer the only debugging evidence.

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-edit-commit-root-list-scenario-run-plan.json`

  - `state_match=true`
  - `semantic_delta_match=true`
  - selected steps: `add-test-todo-type`, `add-test-todo-submit`,
    `edit-test-todo`, `edit-test-todo-change`, `edit-test-todo-commit`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-edit-cancel-root-list-scenario-run-plan.json`

  - `legacy_comparison.passed=true`
  - `state_match=true`
  - `semantic_delta_match=true`

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-edit-blur-root-list-scenario-run-plan.json`

    `edit-test-todo-blur-change`, `edit-test-todo-blur-commit`

Independent review notes:

- Runtime guard reviewer found missing `guard` handling for indexed text/bool
  actions, scalar branch artifact round-trip loss, and two test-only branch
  initializers missing `guard: None`. These are fixed in this follow-up.
- Report-schema reviewer found no indexed replay blocker: guarded indexed
  updates, `TextTrimOrPrevious`, `ReadPath`, `PreviousValue`, `BoolNot`, `Const`,
  and per-event row-resolution cache were present or completed here.
- Report-schema reviewer also found stale durable TodoMVC reports from the prior
  `7c0a1...` plan hash. The new edit reports use `f36ced5...`; older non-edit
  reports still need regeneration before a broad `target/reports/bytes-plan/*`
  aggregate schema run can be claimed.
- Adjacent report-schema limitation remains: root-scalar replay still supports
  only `SourcePayload` and `Const` in that path and will need extension before
  broader guarded/root update parity.

## Phase 7 Follow-up: Source Runtime BYTES Builtin Operation Bodies

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T22:04:59+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-bytes-runtime-operation-audit.json`

Implemented:

- Added RuntimeGeneric whitelist coverage for all current v1 BYTES builtin
  operation names and `Text/to_bytes`.
- Added a shared source-runtime BYTES builtin evaluator used by both
  `eval_runtime_generic_call` and the legacy `eval_call` path. This prevents
  the active RuntimeGeneric path from accepting BYTES calls and then falling
  through to user-function `parse_error`.
- Implemented inline-storage runtime bodies for:
  `Bytes/length`, `Bytes/is_empty`, `Bytes/get`, `Bytes/set`, `Bytes/slice`,
  `Bytes/take`, `Bytes/drop`, `Bytes/concat`, `Bytes/equal`, `Bytes/find`,
  `Bytes/starts_with`, `Bytes/ends_with`, `Bytes/zeros`, `Text/to_bytes`,
  `Bytes/to_text`, `Bytes/from_hex`, `Bytes/to_hex`, `Bytes/from_base64`,
  `Bytes/to_base64`, `Bytes/read_unsigned`, `Bytes/read_signed`,
  `Bytes/write_unsigned`, and `Bytes/write_signed`.
- Added checked offset/range helpers for reads and writes. Runtime OOB,
  malformed hex/base64, invalid UTF-8, invalid endian, invalid byte count, and
  numeric overflow return deterministic `BoonValue::Error` codes instead of
  panicking.
- Numeric read/write paths use explicit `Little`/`Big` endian handling and do
  not depend on host endian.
- BYTES summaries continue to use `report_json()` shape only: storage kind,
  digest, and byte length. Operation-result tests assert `inline_bytes` is not
  present in public runtime summaries.
- Blob/page-backed BYTES operation bodies intentionally return
  `bytes_storage_not_inline` until measured dynamic byte storage lands. This is
  a Phase 7 limitation, not a completed dynamic storage implementation.

Independent review notes:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-bytes-runtime-operation-audit.json`
  records a read-only BYTES runtime audit from agent
  `019edc4b-006c-7241-b81c-36e0ed407fca`.
- The reviewer confirmed parser/typechecker support for the current v1 BYTES
  surface and identified the runtime evaluator body gap in both
  RuntimeGeneric and legacy AST evaluation.
- The reviewer also called out the main risks addressed in this follow-up:
  accidental payload leaks through summaries, implicit TEXT/BYTES coercion,
  unchecked OOB/panics, host-endian dependence, and implementing only one
  evaluator path.
- The reviewer explicitly noted that `File/read_bytes`, `File/write_bytes`,
  typed MachinePlan BYTES operation lowering, and PlanExecutor byte operation
  execution remain open.

Commands run for this follow-up:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo check -p boon_runtime --tests` | Fail, then Pass | First failed because write operations reused an immutable checked-range helper; split mutable and immutable helpers. Final check passed. |
| `cargo fmt --all` | Pass | Applied formatting after edits. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime bytes -- --nocapture` | Fail, Fail, then Pass | First failures were test fixture issues: missing formal `SOURCE`, then missing `HOLD`/`LATEST` usage and typechecker rejection of intentionally invalid literal endian/count. Fixtures were corrected, and exact invalid endian/count assertions moved to direct evaluator tests. Final run passed 9 BYTES-filtered runtime tests. |
| `cargo test -p boon_typecheck bytes -- --nocapture` | Pass | 4 BYTES typechecker tests passed. |
| `cargo test -p boon_parser bytes -- --nocapture` | Pass | 2 BYTES parser tests passed. |
| `cargo test -p boon_plan bytes -- --nocapture` | Pass | 2 BYTES MachinePlan lowering/verifier tests passed. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Formatting check passed after the runtime and ledger edits. |
| `cargo check -p boon_ir -p boon_plan -p boon_runtime -p boon_report_schema -p boon_cli` | Pass | Focused compile for the current BYTES/MachinePlan surface. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-bytes-runtime-operation-audit.json` | Fail, then Pass | First failed because `generated_at_utc` used ISO-8601 while this schema expects Unix seconds. After changing the field to Unix seconds, the report validated; emitted existing `boon_native_gpu` dead-code warnings while compiling `xtask`. |

Exit-gate status for this follow-up:

- Satisfied for source-runtime inline BYTES operation bodies in both
  RuntimeGeneric and legacy dispatch paths.
- Satisfied for focused parser/typechecker/plan/runtime regression coverage of
  the current BYTES surface.
- Not satisfied for full Phase 7 BYTES runtime/storage: measured dynamic byte
  storage, File/read_bytes/write_bytes, typed MachinePlan BYTES operation
  lowering, CPU PlanExecutor byte operation execution, byte source payloads,
  semantic deltas, and zero-allocation fixed-byte normal ticks remain open.
- Not satisfied for Phase 8 example refactors or Phase 9 aggregate/performance
  gates.
- No speedup is claimed. No release benchmark was run.

## Phase 6/9 Follow-up: Generic Root-Scalar Plan Expression Replay

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T21:46:58+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/root_scalar_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/root_scalar_plan_ops.scn`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/root-scalar-plan-ops-dump-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/root-scalar-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`
- Refreshed the maintained targeted reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`.

Implemented:

- Extended non-indexed root-scalar CPU PlanExecutor updates beyond
  `SourcePayload` and `Const`.
- Root update branches now apply typed `PlanSourceGuard` semantics before
  expression evaluation and skip non-matching guarded branches without emitting
  state changes or deltas.
- Root `ReadPath` now evaluates from either a single typed root state input or a
  single typed source-payload input. Ambiguous input sets are runtime/schema
  errors instead of guessed execution.
- Root `PreviousValue`, `BoolNot`, and `TextTrimOrPrevious` now evaluate from
  typed root state/source-payload refs where those refs are unambiguous.
- `run-plan-route` now reuses the generic root-scalar evaluator against initial
  root state instead of maintaining a smaller SourcePayload/Const-only replay
  path.
- `boon_report_schema` mirrors the same evaluator and now parses route
  `source_event` before computing expected route output.
- Added `examples/root_scalar_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/root_scalar_plan_ops.scn` as a durable fixture for root
  `BoolNot`, root-state `ReadPath`, and source-payload `ReadPath`.
- Added runtime and report-schema regression tests covering the fixture shape
  and a forged root source-payload-field report.

Fresh generated reports:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/root-scalar-plan-ops-dump-plan.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `plan_hash=37ac0fd57da37149bbca099874f85b9a2e1cab3355be2fa51e2749c64becb702`
  - `typed_lowering_executable=true`
  - `unresolved_executable_ref_count=0`
  - `executable_string_path_count=0`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/root-scalar-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `plan_hash=37ac0fd57da37149bbca099874f85b9a2e1cab3355be2fa51e2749c64becb702`
  - `legacy_comparison.passed=true`
  - `state_match=true`
  - `semantic_delta_match=true`
  - final root state:
    `store.flag=true`, `store.mirrored="Seed"`,
    `store.copied="Typed payload"`, `store.source_text="Seed"`
  - executed root updates:
    `bool_not`, root-state `read_path`, source-payload `read_path`
  - fallback counters remain zero:
    `runtime_ast_eval_count=0`, `executable_string_path_count=0`,
    `unknown_plan_op_count=0`, `graph_rebuild_count=0`,
    `graph_clones_per_item=0`
- Refreshed TodoMVC reports still use
  `plan_hash=f36ced5be95a9597d2e751230d16be70e8c53325ceb1dc217862aa87ffec812f`
  and the sampled refreshed route/scenario reports passed legacy comparison.

Independent review notes:

- One auditor confirmed `crates/boon_plan` already lowers root update branches
  with typed `ValueRef`s and guards; the gap was isolated to runtime/schema
  root replay.
- A second auditor confirmed the matching runtime/schema limitations:
  root-scalar replay was SourcePayload/Const-only, while indexed replay already
  had the broader guarded evaluator shape.
- Both auditors recommended returning `Option` from root update replay so guard
  misses skip cleanly and passing root state into the evaluator so tick-boundary
  reads use pre-commit state. This follow-up implements that shape.

## Phase 5/6/7/9 Follow-up: Typed PlanExecutor Bytes/length Root Update

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T22:27:43+02:00`

Updated after independent review: `2026-06-18T22:47:27+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/bytes_length_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_length_plan_ops.scn`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-length-plan-ops-dump-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-length-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edc73-369d-7663-b765-ca208a80dd41-bytes-length-plan-runtime-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edc73-4ab2-71a0-a8a9-ea1b3e85532e-bytes-length-report-schema-audit.json`

Implemented:

- Added `UpdateExpression::BytesLength` in semantic IR and recognizer coverage
  for `THEN { payload |> Bytes/length }` and `THEN { Bytes/length(payload) }`
  update branches.
- Fixed root-scalar path canonicalization so unqualified reads inside nested
  store updates first preserve existing child/sibling behavior, but resolve an
  existing top-level field such as `payload` instead of fabricating
  `store.payload`.
- Added `PlanExpressionKind::BytesLength`, typed state-ref collection for the
  BYTES input, and CPU PlanExecutor capability support for the current
  root-scenario executable operation set.
- Tightened `cpu_plan_executor_complete` derivation so root update ops only count
  as CPU-supported when their typed operand shape matches the executor contract:
  source-payload refs, constants, bool inputs/outputs, read-path state/payload
  refs, previous-value refs, text-trim refs, and BYTES-to-NUMBER length refs are
  now checked instead of broadly trusting the expression kind.
- Added CPU PlanExecutor replay for root `Bytes/length`: the executor requires
  exactly one typed state input, verifies the input state summary is BYTES, reads
  `byte_len`, converts it to Boon NUMBER with overflow checking, and emits
  expression kind `bytes_length`.
- Mirrored the same source-derived replay in `boon_report_schema`, including
  forged value rejection and exact legacy-vs-plan semantic delta JSON equality
  for root-scalar scenarios.
- Bound report `binary_hash` to `command_argv[0]` when that command is an
  existing local file, so stale/tampered generated reports are rejected instead
  of treating the hash as advisory.
- Extended the legacy generic runtime enough for honest parity: root source
  routes can now evaluate `BytesLength(path)` as a numeric root update, write it
  as a JSON number, and emit a root numeric semantic `FieldSet`.
- Added durable fixture files:
  `examples/bytes_length_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_length_plan_ops.scn`.
- Added focused tests in `boon_plan`, `boon_runtime`, and
  `boon_report_schema` for the Bytes/length MachinePlan slice and the verifier
  tamper cases found during review.

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-length-plan-ops-dump-plan.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `binary_hash=21e1f3f7d11f5f24863ced4a0f65793649b6c52eba839105da66ca2ee6cb8029`
  - `plan_hash=14ef0feecf5b3984ee5034d6a21bb618a3328ea70eb7115bed035fb80580f654`
  - `typed_lowering_executable=true`
  - `cpu_plan_executor_complete=true`
  - `cpu_plan_executor_unsupported_op_count=0`

  - `unknown_plan_op_count=0`
  - `runtime_ast_dependency_count=0`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-length-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`

  - `plan_hash=14ef0feecf5b3984ee5034d6a21bb618a3328ea70eb7115bed035fb80580f654`
  - `legacy_comparison.passed=true`

    executor update/touched state values remain numeric `4`
  - fallback counters remain zero:
    `runtime_ast_eval_count=0`, `executable_string_path_count=0`,

    `graph_clones_per_item=0`

    covers the tamper.

    schema-invalid historical artifacts. Current review reports for this slice
    are normalized and schema-verified.

Recorded at: `2026-06-18T23:14:32+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Implemented:

- Tightened `run-plan-route` legacy comparison from selected-target/signature
  evidence to exact full semantic-delta JSON equality:
  `legacy_semantic_delta_signatures == plan_semantic_delta_signatures` and
  `legacy_semantic_deltas == plan_semantic_deltas`.
- Changed source-route PlanExecutor proof execution to run the full selected
  source event through the one-step root-scenario executor, then keep
  `target_state` only as an assertion/projection for the selected route.
- Preserved typed validation of the selected source route and target branch:
  route/source id, target state id, selected op id, expression kind, payload
  field, constants, payload schema, and zero fallback counters are still bound.
- Canonicalized scalar route semantic deltas through the root-scenario path, so
  route deltas now match legacy/root-scenario `source_id=null` shape instead of
  leaking the numeric hidden source id into scalar `FieldSet` deltas.
- Expanded source-route executor evidence with `full_state_summary`,
  `semantic_deltas`, `semantic_delta_signatures`, `per_step`, and full execution
  counters from the one-step root-scenario executor.
- Tightened `boon_report_schema` route verification so it recomputes a
  source-derived one-step route scenario from the report `source_event` and
  verifies:
  top-level `semantic_deltas`, executor `semantic_deltas`,
  executor `semantic_delta_signatures`, `full_state_summary`, `per_step`, full
  execution counters, exact legacy/plan delta bodies, and fixed route
  `per_step_pass_fail` ids.
- Added negative schema coverage for forged top-level deltas, forged executor
  delta source ids, extra legacy signatures, and missing
  `legacy_semantic_deltas`.
- Added runtime regression
  `run_plan_route_compares_full_source_event_delta_batch`, where one source
  event updates two root states while the selected route target is only one of
  them. This proves the route proof compares the full event delta batch, not
  just the projected target field.

Implemented:

- Added `UpdateExpression::BytesIsEmpty` in semantic IR with both accepted
  source forms:
  `payload |> Bytes/is_empty` and `Bytes/is_empty(payload)`.
- Added `PlanExpressionKind::BytesIsEmpty` and typed MachinePlan lowering.
  The CPU support predicate requires exactly one typed BYTES state input, one
  source route input, no source-payload refs, no update constant, and a BOOL
  output state.
- Added CPU PlanExecutor root-scalar replay for `Bytes/is_empty`. It reads the
  BYTES state summary `byte_len` and emits a JSON bool `byte_len == 0`.
- Added legacy source-triggered scalar parity support:
  `ScalarUpdateExpression::BytesIsEmpty`, artifact encode/decode, labels,
  root-read tracking, and bool evaluation with a root BYTES reader. This keeps
  compare mode honest instead of relying on a report-only shortcut.
- Mirrored source-derived report-schema replay for
  `PlanExpressionKind::BytesIsEmpty`, including exact BYTES input validation and
  boolean replay from `byte_len`.
- Extended the typechecker BYTES builtin signature test to assert
  `Bytes/is_empty` returns a bool-like type.
- Added `examples/bytes_is_empty_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_is_empty_plan_ops.scn`. One source event updates both an empty
  BYTES root and a filled BYTES root, proving `true` and `false` outcomes in the
  same PlanExecutor scenario.
- Refreshed the two TodoMVC `run-plan-route` reports after the new code relinked
  `boon_cli`, so their `binary_hash` now matches the current local binary.

## Phase 4/5/6/7/9 Follow-up: Typed PlanExecutor Bytes/get Root Updates

Recorded at: `2026-06-19T00:35:20+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/bytes_get_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_get_plan_ops.scn`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-get-plan-ops-dump-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-get-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edca7-3c80-7ed1-b120-fd7d40804330-bytes-get-implementation-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edca7-3dcd-7001-a4d2-97747f3d1149-bytes-get-schema-audit.json`

Implemented:

- Added `InitialValue::Byte { value: u8 }` in semantic IR so root byte
  initializers no longer lower as generic `NUMBER` initial values.
- Added `UpdateExpression::BytesGet { path, index }` in semantic IR with
  recognizers for `payload |> Bytes/get(index: 2)` and
  `Bytes/get(payload, index: 2)`. The current supported index is a literal
  nonnegative integer; dynamic index expressions remain unsupported for this
  slice instead of falling back to string/AST execution.
- Added `PlanValueType::Byte`, `InitialValueKind::Byte`,
  `PlanConstantValue::Byte`, and `PlanExpressionKind::BytesGet`.
- Lowered `Bytes/get` to typed `ValueRef::State` input plus a typed
  `update_constant_id` for the index. The CPU support predicate requires:
  one source trigger, one BYTES state input, no source payload input, a
  nonnegative numeric index constant, zero unresolved refs, and a `BYTE` output
  state.
- Extended MachinePlan verification so `Bytes/get` fails when the index
  constant is missing/invalid or when the output is not `PlanValueType::Byte`.
- Added CPU PlanExecutor root-scalar replay for `Bytes/get`. For this first
  slice it reads inline BYTES from the typed initial plan constant after
  checking the public state summary digest and `byte_len`; it does not claim
  support for mutated dynamic byte banks, blob refs, or page refs.
- Added legacy scalar parity support:
  `ScalarUpdateExpression::BytesGet`, artifact encode/decode, label/root-read
  handling, and numeric evaluation from inline `RuntimeBytes`.
- Added report-schema replay for `Bytes/get`, including private inline-byte
  lookup from the source-derived MachinePlan, summary digest/length validation,
  expected update metadata, and tamper rejection for value/expression/index and
  delta fields.
- Added `examples/bytes_get_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_get_plan_ops.scn`. One source event updates a `BYTE` root
  initialized with `16u00` from `payload |> Bytes/get(index: 2)`, producing
  value `254`.

Architecture decisions and limits:

- This slice does not add `FieldValue::Byte` or `BoonValue::Byte`. Current
  runtime and report boundaries still serialize BYTE values as JSON numbers,
  matching the pre-existing `Bytes/get` builtin behavior. The typed plan now
  prevents accepting a `NUMBER` workaround by requiring `PlanValueType::Byte`
  on the output slot and `expression_kind=bytes_get` in verified reports.
- `Bytes/get` currently supports inline initial BYTES constants in the
  root-scalar PlanExecutor. Dynamic byte banks, byte mutation propagation, blob
  refs, page refs, byte source payloads, and deterministic bounds-error replay
  remain Phase 7 work.
- The index uses `update_constant_id` as typed plan metadata. It is not parsed
  from debug labels or string paths.

Fresh generated reports:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-get-plan-ops-dump-plan.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `binary_hash=88d0f658171c2fa9829c1230e33a2b70fa47c615cb37b8d55cdad86b37f0030c`
  - `plan_hash=36906034477ae959034ee43abe935ca450aa851c531ff709dbc0b02295634aa8`
  - `cpu_plan_executor_complete=true`
  - `cpu_plan_executor_unsupported_op_count=0`
  - `executable_string_path_count=0`
  - `unknown_plan_op_count=0`
  - `machine_plan.storage_layout.scalar_slots[1].value_type.kind=byte`
  - `machine_plan.regions[3].ops[0].kind.update_branch.expression_kind=bytes_get`

## Phase 4/5/6/7/9 Follow-up: Typed PlanExecutor Bytes/equal Root Updates

Recorded at: `2026-06-19T00:14:21+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/bytes_equal_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_equal_plan_ops.scn`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-equal-plan-ops-dump-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-equal-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edcc1-8c4a-7b52-b18e-f8aca96dd11f-bytes-equal-implementation-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edcc1-9f46-7e60-9510-7279d60bdcb0-bytes-equal-schema-audit.json`

Implemented:

- Added `UpdateExpression::BytesEqual { left, right }` in semantic IR.
- Added recognizers for both
  `payload |> Bytes/equal(with: same_payload)` and
  `Bytes/equal(left: payload, right: different_payload)`. This is not new
  Boon syntax; it uses existing pipe/call argument forms and named BYTES state
  operands.
- Added `PlanExpressionKind::BytesEqual`.
- Lowered both equality operands to typed `ValueRef::State` inputs. The CPU
  support predicate requires one source trigger, exactly two distinct BYTES
  state inputs, a BOOL output state, no source payload inputs, no update
  constant, no unresolved executable refs, and a non-indexed op.
- Extended MachinePlan verification so `Bytes/equal` rejects an unexpected
  update constant or non-BOOL output.
- Added CPU PlanExecutor root-scalar replay for `Bytes/equal`. The current
  slice reads inline initial BYTES constants through the same digest/length
  checked path as `Bytes/get` and compares byte arrays, not public summaries.
- Added legacy scalar parity support:
  `ScalarUpdateExpression::BytesEqual`, artifact encode/decode, symbol
  interning, root-read tracking, classifier exhaustiveness, kind labels, and
  boolean evaluation from inline `RuntimeBytes`.
- Added report-schema replay for `Bytes/equal`, including a two-state-input
  helper and tamper checks for update value, state summary value, expression
  kind, source payload field, update constant id/value, semantic delta value,
  legacy semantic delta value, and update count.
- Added `examples/bytes_equal_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_equal_plan_ops.scn`. One source event updates two BOOL roots:
  `store.same=true` for equal BYTES[4] operands and `store.different=false` for
  same-length unequal BYTES[4] operands.

Architecture decisions and limits:

- `Bytes/equal` support requires two distinct state inputs. A self-comparison
  that collapses to one unique typed input is intentionally not accepted by this
  root-scalar MachinePlan slice, because it would hide missing binary operand
  metadata.
- The fixture uses named BYTES state operands, not inline BYTES literals inside
  `with:`. Typed constant operands for BYTES operation arguments remain a later
  lowering/storage task.
- This slice does not implement dynamic/mutated byte banks, blob refs, page
  refs, byte source payloads, File/read_bytes/write_bytes, or zero-allocation
  fixed-byte ticks. Those remain Phase 7 work.
- No default execution switch happened. Legacy execution remains available and
  is used only for compare-mode parity evidence.

## Phase 4/5/6/7/9 Follow-up: Typed `Bytes/concat` Root Scalar Slice

Recorded at: `2026-06-19`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `examples/bytes_concat_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_concat_plan_ops.scn`

Implemented:

- Added `UpdateExpression::BytesConcat { left, right }` in `boon_ir`.
- Recognized both `left |> Bytes/concat(with: right)` and
  `Bytes/concat(input: left, with: right)` in update branches.
- Added `PlanExpressionKind::BytesConcat`.
- Added typed `ordered_inputs` metadata to `PlanOpKind::UpdateBranch`. This is
  required because `PlanOp.inputs` is intentionally unique/sorted and cannot
  preserve non-commutative operand order.
- MachinePlan support for `Bytes/concat` now requires:
  - one source trigger;
  - no source payload field;
  - no update constant;
  - two distinct ordered `ValueRef::State` operands;
  - both ordered inputs declared in `op.inputs`;
  - both operands typed `PlanValueType::Bytes`;
  - output typed `PlanValueType::Bytes`;
  - fixed output length equal to `left.fixed_len + right.fixed_len` when all
    three lengths are known.
- Added CPU PlanExecutor root-scalar replay for `Bytes/concat`. It reads
  source-derived inline BYTES operands, concatenates them in `ordered_inputs`
  order, checks fixed output length, and writes the standard public BYTES
  summary.
- Added legacy root BYTES commit support so compare-mode parity can report
  real BYTES root semantic deltas instead of dropping or textifying them:
  `GenericRootBytesCommit`, `ProtocolValue::Bytes(JsonValue)`, root BYTES
  source-action dispatch, `RuntimeStorage::set_root_bytes`, and
  `ScalarEquationPlan::eval_bytes`.
- Added report-schema replay for `Bytes/concat`. Schema verification derives
  bytes from source-derived typed input constants, recomputes digest/length,
  and compares state summaries, update values, semantic deltas, and legacy
  deltas against that derived value.
- Added `examples/bytes_concat_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_concat_plan_ops.scn`. The fixture has two fixed BYTES roots
  (`BYTES[3] { 16u01, 16u02, 16u03 }` and `BYTES[2] { 16uFE, 16u04 }`) and two
  explicit zero-filled `BYTES[5] {}` outputs, covering pipe and call forms.

Architecture decisions and limits:

- `Bytes/concat` is the first BYTES-producing MachinePlan operation in this
  root-scalar slice. Because operand order is semantic, ordered typed operands
  are now explicit MachinePlan data instead of inferred from the unordered
  `inputs` set.
- The current slice requires two distinct BYTES state inputs. Self-concat can
  be added later once the plan can represent repeated operand positions without
  collapsing them through unique input sets.
- Public reports still do not include inline output bytes. They include
  `$boon_type`, `storage`, `digest`, and `byte_len`; schema replay derives the
  expected bytes privately from typed source constants.
- Chained BYTES operations that consume a mutated concat output are still not
  implemented because `root_state_inline_bytes` can only recover bytes from
  typed initial constants. This remains Phase 7 private byte-bank/dynamic
  storage work.
- This slice does not implement byte source payloads, typed BYTES constants as
  operation operands, blob/page byte reads, File/read_bytes/write_bytes, or
  zero-allocation fixed-byte ticks.
- No default execution switch happened. Legacy execution remains available and
  is used only for compare-mode parity evidence.

  - Critical finding resolved: concat operand order cannot be inferred from
    unique/sorted `op.inputs`.
  - High finding resolved: generic runtime builtin existed, but typed
    IR/MachinePlan/PlanExecutor root-scalar support did not.
  - High finding resolved: legacy compare mode needed real BYTES root commit

    inputs.
  - High finding resolved: schema rejects forged concat values and metadata.
  - High finding resolved: MachinePlan support requires two distinct typed
    BYTES operands and a BYTES output.
  - Medium finding deferred: schema replay of later BYTES ops consuming mutated
    concat output needs private byte-store evidence.

- Satisfied for ordered operand preservation, explicit zero-filled fixed

- Satisfied for independent implementation/schema review artifacts for this

  refactors, full TodoMVC scenario parity, Cells PlanExecutor parity, release

## Phase 6/7/9 Follow-up: Private BYTES State For Chained PlanExecutor Steps

Recorded at: `2026-06-19`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Contract note:

- `/home/martinkavik/Downloads/BOON_BYTES_AND_MACHINEPLAN_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`
  currently has SHA-256
  `353f0fd0731754a2ab8c3844c922177439035da774c033f479f0368969a3b335`
  and starts with the older generic `# Implementation Plan`, not the earlier
  BYTES/MachinePlan plan hash recorded when this ledger was created. This
  follow-up therefore used the active `/goal` objective text as the concrete
  contract and recorded the mismatch instead of silently weakening scope.

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `examples/bytes_concat_chain_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_concat_chain_plan_ops.scn`

Implemented:

- Added private root BYTES state in the CPU root-scalar PlanExecutor:
  `RootBytesState = BTreeMap<usize, RuntimeBytes>`.
- `plan_root_scalar_initial_state` now initializes both public root JSON state
  and private BYTES state from typed inline BYTES constants, and verifies the
  public summary matches the private payload.
- `root_state_inline_bytes` now reads through private BYTES state first and
  verifies the public `$boon_type`/`storage`/`digest`/`byte_len` summary before
  returning inline bytes. It still has the initial-constant fallback for typed
  constants.
- `ExecutedRootUpdate` now carries `bytes_value: Option<RuntimeBytes>`.
  `Bytes/concat` produces both the public BYTES summary and private payload;
  root commits update the private sidecar when a BYTES-producing branch commits.
- `Bytes/length`, `Bytes/is_empty`, `Bytes/get`, `Bytes/equal`, and
  `Bytes/concat` all consume bytes through the same validated private-state
  path.
- `ReadPath` and `PreviousValue` preserve private bytes when copying a BYTES
  state, so aliases do not lose payload materialization.
- Report-schema replay mirrors the runtime design with
  `ExpectedRootBytesState = BTreeMap<usize, Vec<u8>>`,
  `ExpectedRootScalarUpdate.private_bytes`, private-state commits, and validated
  public summaries.
- Added `examples/bytes_concat_chain_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_concat_chain_plan_ops.scn`. The fixture:
  - Step `join-bytes`: writes `store.joined` from
    `left_payload |> Bytes/concat(with: right_payload)`.
  - Step `inspect-joined`: consumes the committed `store.joined` with
    `Bytes/length`, `Bytes/get(index: 3)`, and another `Bytes/concat`.

Architecture decisions and limits:

- This is committed-step chaining. It proves state produced in one source tick
  can be consumed by a later source tick. It does not claim same-source-event
  chaining, because current root updates intentionally batch candidates and
  commit at the tick boundary. Same-event dependencies require a future ordered
  scheduler/toposort design.
- Public reports still do not expose inline bytes. They expose only BYTES
  summaries with `storage`, `digest`, and `byte_len`; private bytes stay inside
  the executor/schema replay.
- The sidecar is an incremental Phase 7 step toward typed byte banks. It is not
  the final measured fixed/dynamic byte-bank design, allocator strategy,
  `bytes::Bytes`/arena profile, byte source payload support, or File boundary.
- Existing numeric semantic deltas remain stringified by
  `root_plan_semantic_delta_value`/legacy parity for current report shape; this
  slice did not redesign semantic delta number encoding.

## Phase 4/5/6/7/9 Follow-up: Bytes/set Typed Mutation Slice

Recorded at: `2026-06-19`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `examples/bytes_set_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_set_plan_ops.scn`

Implemented:

- Added `UpdateExpression::BytesSet { path, index, value }` to semantic IR.
- Added pipe and call-form extraction for literal `Bytes/set` updates:
  - `payload |> Bytes/set(index: 2, value: 16uAA)`
  - `Bytes/set(input: payload, index: 2, value: 16uAA)`
- Added `PlanExpressionKind::BytesSet`.
- Added `ValueRef::Constant(PlanConstantId)` so typed plan operands can include
  constants in ordered operation inputs without converting back to strings.
- Lowered `Bytes/set` to ordered typed operands:
  `[State(BYTES input), Constant(Number index), Constant(Byte value)]`.
- Kept legacy single-constant `update_constant_id` behavior for existing
  one-constant operations. For `Bytes/set`, reports use
  `update_constant_id=[index_constant_id, value_constant_id]` and
  `update_constant_value={"index": ..., "value": ...}`.
- MachinePlan verification now rejects malformed `Bytes/set` plans when:
  - the input operand is not a typed BYTES state,
  - either constant operand is missing,
  - the index constant is not a nonnegative number,
  - the value constant is not a BYTE,
  - a fixed-length BYTES input has a statically out-of-bounds index,
  - the output is not BYTES,
  - fixed input/output lengths differ.
- CPU root-scalar PlanExecutor now executes `Bytes/set` by reading validated
  private BYTES state, checking bounds, copying and mutating one byte, and
  committing both public summary and private bytes.
- Legacy scalar equation evaluation now supports `Bytes/set`, so compare mode
  can prove exact state and semantic-delta parity.
- Report-schema replay mirrors the runtime `Bytes/set` semantics, recomputes
  private bytes, verifies public BYTES summaries, and rejects forged evidence.
- Added `examples/bytes_set_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_set_plan_ops.scn`. The fixture:
  - Step `patch-bytes`: writes `store.patched` from
    `left_payload |> Bytes/set(index: 2, value: 16uAA)`.
  - Step `inspect-patched`: consumes committed `store.patched` with
    `Bytes/get(index: 2)`, `Bytes/length`, and `Bytes/concat`.

Architecture decisions and limits:

- `Bytes/set` currently supports literal index and byte value operands in the
  typed HOLD-update path. Broader expression-valued indexes/bytes remain future
  typed operation work.
- Multi-constant operations use positional typed ordered operands for this
  slice, matching the existing `Bytes/concat` ordered-input design. A later
  cleanup should introduce role-tagged operands, such as `BytesIndex` and
  `BytesValue`, to make reports and verifiers less position-dependent.
- Public reports still do not expose inline bytes. They expose only BYTES
  summaries with `storage`, `digest`, and `byte_len`; private bytes remain in
  runtime/schema replay.
- This is committed-step chaining. It does not claim same-source-event
  dependency scheduling.
- This is still inline sidecar storage, not final measured fixed/dynamic byte
  banks, byte source payloads, File/read_bytes/write_bytes, allocator profiling,
  or zero-allocation fixed-byte tick proof.

## Phase 4/5/6/7/9 Follow-up: Typed `Bytes/slice`, `Bytes/take`, and `Bytes/drop` Root Scalar Slice

Base/current commit:

- `0f891e5`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/bytes_slice_take_drop_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_slice_take_drop_plan_ops.scn`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-slice-take-drop-plan-ops-dump-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-slice-take-drop-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edd26-b510-7b12-9463-c669ca8bd8be-bytes-slice-take-drop-implementation-map.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edd26-b68e-7832-92f3-9c6288721200-bytes-slice-take-drop-adversarial-audit.json`

What changed:

- Added typed semantic IR variants and recognizers for `Bytes/slice`,
  `Bytes/take`, and `Bytes/drop`.
- Added MachinePlan expression kinds, typed ordered operands, static
  fixed-length and bounds verification, and CPU PlanExecutor capability
  support for the three operations.
- Added runtime scalar-expression artifact round trips, legacy compare-mode
  evaluation, typed root PlanExecutor execution, checked range arithmetic,
  fixed output length checks, and private BYTES sidecar updates.
- Added report-schema replay for the three operations so expected state,
  semantic deltas, expression kinds, constants, and private bytes are
  recomputed instead of trusted from JSON.
- Extended the BYTES typechecker builtin smoke for slice/take/drop.
- Added the focused fixture
  `examples/bytes_slice_take_drop_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_slice_take_drop_plan_ops.scn`. The fixture:
  - Step `split-bytes`: creates `store.sliced`, `store.taken`, and
    `store.dropped` from `payload`.
  - Step `inspect-splits`: consumes committed private BYTES state through
    `Bytes/length`, `Bytes/get`, and `Bytes/concat`.

Architecture decisions and limits:

- `Bytes/slice`, `Bytes/take`, and `Bytes/drop` support literal numeric
  offsets/counts in the typed HOLD-update path for this slice.
  Expression-valued offsets/counts remain future typed-operation work.
- Ordered operands are required for these operations because `op.inputs` is a
  normalized dependency set and cannot preserve the semantic order of
  `offset` and `byte_count`.
- Static verifier rejects known out-of-bounds ranges for fixed-size inputs
  before execution. Runtime and schema replay still use checked range
  arithmetic for dynamic cases and tampered plans.
- Public reports still expose only BYTES summaries (`storage`, `digest`,
  `byte_len`); produced inline bytes remain in private runtime/schema state.
- This is committed-step chaining, not same-source-event dependency scheduling.
- This does not implement final fixed/dynamic byte banks, File/read_bytes,
  File/write_bytes, byte SOURCE payloads, broader BYTES operations, release
  benchmarks, or the default PlanExecutor switch.

## Phase 4/5/6/7/9 Follow-up: Typed `Bytes/find`, `Bytes/starts_with`, and `Bytes/ends_with` Root Scalar Slice

Base/current commit:

- `0f891e5`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/bytes_search_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_search_plan_ops.scn`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-search-plan-ops-dump-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-search-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edd3d-f33e-78f2-b9b5-d106ed548144-bytes-search-implementation-map.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edd3e-04b7-76b1-a14c-8811c9480142-bytes-search-adversarial-audit.json`

What changed:

- Added focused typechecker coverage for `Bytes/find`,
  `Bytes/starts_with`, and `Bytes/ends_with`.
- Added an IR lowering regression proving both pipe and call forms become
  typed `UpdateExpression` variants with the expected state paths.
- Added MachinePlan verifier coverage for ordered two-state BYTES operands,
  output type checks, zero unsupported ops, and metadata tamper rejection.
- Added report-schema replay for the three operations so expected values are
  recomputed from private BYTES state instead of trusted from JSON.
- Fixed `Bytes/find` miss parity. Legacy `NaN` appears as JSON `null` in
  canonical state summaries, so the CPU PlanExecutor now emits `null` for a
  miss. The scheduled scalar route now carries `GenericRootNumberValue::Null`
  so a miss is a valid update, not a skipped numeric branch.
- Added the focused fixture `examples/bytes_search_plan_ops.bn` and
  `examples/bytes_search_plan_ops.scn`. The fixture:
  - Step `build-bytes`: creates `store.joined` through `Bytes/concat`.
  - Step `measure-bytes`: consumes committed private BYTES state through
    `Bytes/find`, `Bytes/starts_with`, and `Bytes/ends_with`.
  - Covers found index, miss/null, empty needle, true and false prefix, and
    true and false suffix.

Architecture decisions and limits:

- Search/predicate operations use ordered state operands because `op.inputs`
  is a normalized dependency set and cannot preserve semantic operand order.
- Unlike `Bytes/concat`, search/predicate operations are allowed to use the
  same BYTES state as both operands because `find`, `starts_with`, and
  `ends_with` are well-defined for that case.
- `Bytes/find` miss maps to canonical report/state `null`, matching legacy
  `BoonValue::NaN` state-summary serialization.
- Public reports still expose only BYTES summaries (`storage`, `digest`,
  `byte_len`); the search fixture proves search reads private runtime/schema
  byte state produced by an earlier `Bytes/concat` tick.
- This does not add expression-valued operands, File/read_bytes,
  File/write_bytes, byte source payloads, final dynamic byte banks, broader
  BYTES operation matrices, release benchmarks, or the default PlanExecutor
  switch.

## Phase 4/5/6/7/9 Follow-up: Typed BYTES Numeric Read/Write Root Scalar Slice

Date: 2026-06-19

Base/current commit:

- `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
  - Added focused IR regression coverage for `Bytes/read_unsigned`,
    `Bytes/read_signed`, `Bytes/write_unsigned`, and `Bytes/write_signed`
    pipe/call forms in the numeric fixture.
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
  - Added focused MachinePlan regression coverage for numeric ordered
    operands and verifier rejection of an unsupported endian constant.
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
  - Added PlanExecutor compare-mode regression coverage for numeric reads,
    writes, later inspection of written bytes, public BYTES summaries, and
    zero fallback counters.
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
  - Added schema replay/tamper regression coverage for numeric expression
    kinds, digests, read values, and update constants.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edd6d-959d-7be2-91a4-15b2adf841d8-bytes-numeric-implementation-map.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edd6d-9708-7df1-a1b4-46e5c73d3538-bytes-numeric-adversarial-audit.json`

Architecture decisions and limits:

- Numeric BYTES operations use typed MachinePlan ordered operands:
  - reads: BYTES state, offset constant, byte_count constant, endian constant
  - writes: BYTES state, offset constant, byte_count constant, endian
    constant, value constant
- Endian remains canonical in typed plans: `Little` or `Big`. Legacy runtime
  aliases are not accepted as typed MachinePlan constants in this slice.
- Offset, byte_count, endian, and write value are literal MachinePlan
  constants in this first numeric slice. Dynamic numeric operands are still
  deferred until MachinePlan has an expression/value-ref operand design for
  these builtin arguments.
- Public reports continue to expose only BYTES summaries (`storage`, `digest`,
  `byte_len`). The fixture proves writes create real runtime/schema BYTES
  state by reading written bytes in a later source tick.
- This does not add File/read_bytes, File/write_bytes, byte source payloads,
  dynamic byte banks, expression-valued numeric operands, full negative
  source fixtures, release benchmarks, or the default PlanExecutor switch.

  - High finding addressed: typed implementation had to be proven through IR,
    MachinePlan, PlanExecutor, and report-schema replay, not only parser or
    legacy runtime support.
  - High finding addressed: numeric writes must produce BYTES state that later
    byte operations can consume.
  - Medium finding addressed: canonical endian constants and byte_count
    operands are explicit in typed ordered inputs.
  - Medium finding deferred: dynamic numeric operands remain future
    MachinePlan expression/value-ref work.
  - Medium finding deferred: broad negative fixture matrices remain Phase 9
    work.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edd6d-9708-7df1-a1b4-46e5c73d3538-bytes-numeric-adversarial-audit.json`
  - report SHA-256:
    `0db88730462f3d5cfd33d1aae3969880556b289fb18c2aa0180d9526f6bd88b5`
  - Critical finding addressed: numeric BYTES support could be
    variant-shaped but not implementation-shaped.
  - High finding addressed: dump-plan evidence must prove CPU PlanExecutor
    completeness and zero unsupported-op counters.
  - High finding addressed: report-schema replay must include numeric BYTES
    branches and tamper rejection.
  - High finding addressed: write operations must be proven by later reads
    from runtime/schema byte state, not only public summaries.
  - Medium finding partially addressed: signed, endian, range, and value-bound
    positive coverage exists for this fixture.
  - Medium finding deferred: full source-level negative fixtures for every
    byte_count, overflow, OOB, and fixed-length failure remain Phase 9 work.

## Phase 9 Follow-up: BYTES Negative Gate and Numeric MachinePlan Rejection Matrix

Date: 2026-06-19

Base/current commit:

- `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/xtask/Cargo.toml`
  - Added direct `boon_plan` and `boon_typecheck` dependencies for the
    BYTES/MachinePlan negative gate.
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
  - Added `verify-bytes-negative`.
  - The gate writes invalid source/scenario fixtures under
    `target/generated/bytes-negative-fixtures/`, validates parser/typechecker
    and PlanExecutor rejection paths, generates a fresh positive BYTES
    `run-plan-root-scalar-scenario` base report, mutates cloned report copies,
    asserts report-schema rejects those mutations, deletes the temporary
    `_negative-bytes-*` reports, and writes one durable pass report.
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
  - Added `bytes_numeric_plan_verifier_rejects_invalid_operands_and_output_lengths`
    covering invalid numeric byte_count, fixed input range OOB, unsigned write
    overflow, signed write overflow, and numeric write output fixed-length
    mismatch.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edd88-86cf-78d0-bcb5-dd54a016a5ba-bytes-negative-language-plan-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edd88-8839-7160-bae6-1115d452fb0f-bytes-negative-gate-audit.json`

Architecture decisions and limits:

- `verify-bytes-negative` is a normal pass report. No BYTES-specific
  report-schema exception or blocker-audit bypass was added.
- The gate validates a fresh positive BYTES report before relying on mutated
  negative-report rejection, so stale base evidence cannot make all mutations
  pass for the wrong reason.
- Malformed report clones are temporary and removed through `schema_rejects`;
  a post-run check found no `_negative-bytes-*.json` files left under
  `target/reports/bytes-plan`.
- The first durable gate covers 18 negative cases. It is not the complete
  Phase 9 negative matrix.
- The gate records rejection booleans and details, but it does not yet capture
  exact schema rejection error text per tampered report.

## Phase 7/8/9 Follow-up: Sandboxed Build `File/read_bytes` Boundary

Date: 2026-06-19

Base/current commit:

- `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `examples/todo_mvc_physical/BUILD.bn`
- `examples/novywave/BUILD.bn`
- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edd98-3a06-7982-b68d-16a0cdc50962-phase8-example-refactor-scout.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edd98-38d2-70b2-baf4-5df4163b10e8-file-bytes-implementation-scout.json`

Implemented:

- `File/read_bytes` is registered as a TEXT-pipe builtin returning dynamic
  BYTES.
- `File/read_text` is registered as a TEXT-pipe builtin returning TEXT so the
  old and new host-boundary forms have explicit type signatures.
- Physical TodoMVC and NovyWave build files now ingest SVG assets with:
  `File/read_bytes() |> Bytes/to_text(encoding: Utf8)`.
- The sandboxed build runner reads physical asset files as bytes, records byte
  length, SHA-256, and UTF-8 decode evidence, then decodes to text before the
  existing URL-encoded TEXT asset generation.
- `BuildExecutionResult` now reports `input_byte_reads`.
- Added `cargo xtask verify-build-bytes-boundary` to validate physical
  TodoMVC/NovyWave build files, byte-read evidence, generated output parity,
  source/build hashes, and absence of legacy `File/read_text()` in these build
  files.

Architecture decisions and limits:

- This is a build-boundary step only. It is not the typed MachinePlan
  `File/read_bytes` operation required by full Phase 7.
- Existing generated asset modules still contain TEXT data URLs because the
  renderer contract currently consumes those strings. Final BYTES-backed asset
  descriptors remain deferred.
- `File/write_bytes` is not implemented. It needs a typed host-effect and
  result contract before it can be accepted.
- Cells formula byte scanning is not implemented in this slice because the
  current Cells scenario still has a known semantic baseline failure at
  `commit-c0-formula-bar-sum-through-a3`, and a byte scanner must preserve
  formula dependency tracking rather than hide it.
- No performance or speedup is claimed. No release benchmark was run.

## Phase 4/5/6/7/8/9 Follow-up: Typed MachinePlan `File/read_bytes`

Date: `2026-06-19`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Changed files in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `examples/bytes_file_read_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_file_read_plan_ops.scn`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edda5-b7b4-7c92-9273-15cf60677239-file-read-plan-scout.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edda5-d0c7-7eb0-a843-4552b13374fe-file-read-verification-audit.json`

Implementation:

- Added `UpdateExpression::FileReadBytes` and `PlanExpressionKind::FileReadBytes`.
- `boon_ir` recognizes static `TEXT { ... } |> File/read_bytes()` and
  call-form static path arguments before generic read-path fallback.
- `InitialValue::Bytes` now preserves dynamic vs fixed length metadata. This
  fixes the earlier engine bug where `BYTES {}` lowered to fixed `BYTES[0]`
  and rejected a dynamic host-file read result.
- `boon_plan` lowers `File/read_bytes` as a typed update branch with one static
  TEXT constant path operand, a BYTES output state, no state inputs, and no
  source-payload inputs.
- `boon_runtime` root-scalar PlanExecutor resolves the file path relative to
  the Boon source parent directory, rejects absolute and parent-directory path
  components, canonicalizes the resolved file, rejects symlink/root escapes,
  reads bytes, commits dynamic BYTES, and reports digest/length only.
- Added `examples/bytes_file_read_plan_ops.bn` and `.scn`, which read
  `examples/todo_mvc_physical/assets/icons/checkbox_active.svg` into BYTES,
  then consume the committed file BYTES through later `Bytes/length` and
  `Bytes/get` updates.
- Added `cargo xtask verify-bytes-file-read-plan`, a focused gate that compiles
  the source-derived MachinePlan, runs the PlanExecutor, verifies zero fallback
  counters, binds the read file path/length/SHA-256/first byte, and writes a
  pass report.
- Added a dedicated `verify-bytes-file-read-plan` report-schema branch. The
  branch recompiles the source-derived MachinePlan, compares plan hash/version/
  capability summary, verifies exactly one `FileReadBytes` op, checks selected
  scenario steps, verifies the host file artifact bytes, requires the follow-up
  `Bytes/length` and `Bytes/get` state values, requires zero fallback counters,
  and rejects public `inline_bytes` leakage.

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
  - report SHA-256:
    `e137daa149129739b69281c0daac0e157e3af0512f9c478d3cc28cf39f321553`

  - `source_hash=e5cc8da332317a0f58ad5ef31172b2d7a15e7b2139d9ad4a937f8fb45d2b2adb`

  - `graph_node_count=24`
  - `negative_case_count=22`

    - source path literal:
      `todo_mvc_physical/assets/icons/checkbox_active.svg`

Exit-gate status for this follow-up:

- Satisfied for a narrow typed MachinePlan `File/read_bytes` root-scalar
  operation with a static TEXT path literal.
- Satisfied for dynamic `BYTES {}` PlanValueType preservation at the
  MachinePlan output state.
- Satisfied for CPU PlanExecutor host-file read execution under source-relative
  path sandboxing.
- Satisfied for follow-up BYTES consumers reading the committed file BYTES
  state through `Bytes/length` and `Bytes/get`.
- Satisfied for a command-specific schema branch that rejects generic
  pass-through and binds source/scenario/plan/file artifacts.
- Satisfied for report-level adversarial stale/tamper rejection for the 22
  `File/read_bytes` mutation cases listed above.
- Not satisfied for dynamic path operands, typed `File/write_bytes`, byte source
  payloads, measured byte banks, same-event dependency scheduling, fixed-byte
  zero-allocation proof, or broad no-panic/OOB proof.
- Not satisfied for final TodoMVC/NovyWave BYTES asset descriptor integration
  or Cells formula byte scanning.
- Not satisfied for live source/runtime negative fixtures for every
  `File/read_bytes` host-path and type rejection mode.
- No speedup is claimed. No release benchmark was run.

### Addendum: live `File/read_bytes` source/runtime negative fixtures

Date: `2026-06-19`

Adaptation note:

- The objective names `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_IMPLEMENTATION.md` as
  the primary contract, but that file is not present in the current worktree.
  This slice followed the pasted objective text, AGENTS.md, current code, and
  this progress ledger. The missing plan file remains an artifact-location gap,
  not a scope reduction.

Changed files in this addendum:

- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019eddd3-72b9-73b0-8be4-878321770904-file-read-live-negative-runtime-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019eddd3-8704-7ef3-ab8e-5de356ac18ad-file-read-live-negative-schema-audit.json`

Implementation:

- `cargo xtask verify-bytes-file-read-plan` now generates and executes live
  `File/read_bytes` negative fixtures under
  `target/generated/bytes-file-read-live-negative-fixtures/`.
- The live fixtures are separate from mutated-report negatives:
  - `live_negative_case_count=9`
  - `live_negative_cases` records each case's source/scenario path and SHA-256,
    target profile, selected steps, stage, category, expected error fragment,
    actual error detail, actual error SHA-256, and target kind when relevant.
- The live fixture matrix currently covers:
  - `source-empty-path`: semantic IR rejects empty static path.
  - `source-absolute-path`: semantic IR rejects absolute path.
  - `source-parent-directory-path`: semantic IR rejects parent-directory path.
  - `source-dynamic-path`: semantic IR rejects dynamic path because current v1
    `File/read_bytes` only lowers static TEXT paths.
  - `runtime-missing-file`: PlanExecutor rejects a missing source-relative file.
  - `runtime-directory-target`: PlanExecutor rejects directory targets.
  - `runtime-fixed-output-length-mismatch`: PlanExecutor rejects reading four
    bytes into fixed one-byte output.
  - `plan-wrong-output-type`: typechecker rejects writing BYTES into a TEXT
    `HOLD`.
  - `runtime-symlink-escape`: PlanExecutor rejects a source-root symlink whose
    canonical target escapes the source root.
- `boon_report_schema` now requires the exact live negative case matrix,
  validates source/scenario hashes, validates artifact hash entries, validates
  expected error fragments, validates actual error hashes, validates filesystem
  target kinds (`missing`, `directory`, `file`, `symlink_escape`), and requires
  passing `live-negative-*-rejected` checks.
- The report-mutation matrix now has 25 cases. The three added cases reject:
  - missing live negative evidence,
  - forged live negative expected error,
  - forged live negative target kind.

    `cc64539c0ccc64983096c6958df5fd9474c0dda2173802b6c2d3e4f62a0122f8`
  - `status=pass`
  - `negative_case_count=25`
  - `live_negative_case_count=9`
  - `per_step_pass_fail` count `50`

### Addendum: fabricated `File/read_bytes` MachinePlan negative fixtures

Date: `2026-06-19`

Changed files in this addendum:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edddf-a344-7f70-b00e-8eb50f781bc2-file-read-fabricated-plan-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edddf-e1b9-7e20-ba74-2ed9e96722f9-file-read-fabricated-schema-audit.json`
- Refreshed `artifact_sha256s` in the four older File/read_bytes subagent
  reports so they bind to the regenerated main report hash.

Implementation:

- Added an explicit `file-read-bytes-ops-well-formed` MachinePlan verifier
  check in `boon_plan::verify_plan`.
- The verifier requires every `PlanExpressionKind::FileReadBytes` update branch
  to have:
  - no `source_payload_field`,
  - no `update_constant_id`,
  - BYTES output storage,
  - exactly one source input,
  - no source-payload or state inputs,
  - exactly one ordered input,
  - an ordered input that resolves to a static safe TEXT path constant.
- `cargo xtask verify-bytes-file-read-plan` now fabricates malformed
  MachinePlans by cloning the source-derived accepted plan, mutating the
  `FileReadBytes` op, hashing the mutated plan, and running
  `boon_plan::verify_plan`.
- The fabricated matrix has 12 verifier-only cases:
  - `fabricated-missing-path-operand`
  - `fabricated-extra-path-operand`
  - `fabricated-missing-path-constant`
  - `fabricated-non-text-path-constant`
  - `fabricated-non-static-parent-path`
  - `fabricated-missing-source-input`
  - `fabricated-extra-source-input`
  - `fabricated-state-input-declared`
  - `fabricated-source-payload-input-declared`
  - `fabricated-update-constant-present`
  - `fabricated-source-payload-field-present`
  - `fabricated-output-not-bytes`
- Each fabricated case records `evidence_level=verifier-only`,
  `runtime_executed=false`, `base_plan_hash`, `mutated_plan_hash`,
  `fabricated_plan_hash`, full `boon_plan_verification`, and exact failed check
  IDs. These cases are not runtime evidence.
- `boon_report_schema` now independently replays every fabricated mutation from
  the embedded source-derived MachinePlan, recomputes the mutated plan hash,
  reruns `boon_plan::verify_plan`, compares full verification JSON, enforces the
  exact fabricated case matrix and array length, and rejects unsupported keys in
  fabricated cases.
- The report-mutation matrix now has 31 cases. The six fabricated-plan-specific
  report mutations reject:
  - missing fabricated plan negative evidence,
  - forged fabricated plan hash,
  - forged fabricated expected failed checks,
  - forged `runtime_executed=true`,
  - runtime-looking keys smuggled into fabricated cases,
  - duplicated fabricated case objects.

Fresh generated report:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
  - report SHA-256:
    `bee33bfea91d1a790a3dfd258a4beea224b246b45e2a33715ff9d6941139d971`
  - plan hash:
    `69e627900c6f658895386d1d705afa45bda8e3a97ce9fe6b4c6ba2ef1c1156db`
  - `status=pass`
  - `negative_case_count=31`
  - `live_negative_case_count=9`
  - `fabricated_plan_negative_case_count=12`
  - `per_step_pass_fail` count `68`

Independent review reports:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edddf-a344-7f70-b00e-8eb50f781bc2-file-read-fabricated-plan-audit.json`
  - report SHA-256:
    `32a29a48667f71f38075f40264674fa6ac4ef1653b52f861d05b0d20ac64a65d`
  - Low finding addressed: missing/extra source-route input mutations were
    added to the fabricated matrix.
  - High findings addressed: all fabricated cases are real MachinePlan
    mutations and all final cases fail `file-read-bytes-ops-well-formed` with
    `runtime_executed=false`.
  - No critical/high issue remains in this focused audit.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edddf-e1b9-7e20-ba74-2ed9e96722f9-file-read-fabricated-schema-audit.json`
  - report SHA-256:
    `904e31bd5419324a2e5e7b2f9850629f5e0ce5205c484ab15099e4cd2fb03bf3`
  - Medium finding addressed: fabricated cases now reject runtime-looking key
    smuggling.
  - Low finding addressed: duplicated fabricated case objects are rejected.
  - High finding addressed: schema replay rejects forged hashes, expected check
    IDs, and verification status.
  - No critical/high issue remains in this focused audit.
- Older related review reports were refreshed to bind to the regenerated main

### Addendum: typed MachinePlan `File/write_bytes` static-path host effect

Date: `2026-06-19`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Changed files in this addendum:

- `crates/boon_parser/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede0e-35ed-7891-9aae-88ef5c3884e7-file-write-runtime-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede0e-6fcf-7b93-93c5-c25c92534cd8-file-write-schema-audit.json`

Implementation:

- Added `File/write_bytes` to the known Boon function surface.
- Typechecker behavior for this narrow slice:
  - piped input to `File/write_bytes` is `BYTES`,
  - `path:` / positional path argument is `TEXT`,
  - v1 return is `TEXT`, currently the written relative path status.
- Added `UpdateExpression::FileWriteBytes { bytes_path, path }` and detection
  for `THEN { bytes |> File/write_bytes(path: TEXT { ... }) }`.
- IR validation rejects empty, absolute, and parent-directory write paths and
  requires the byte input path to resolve as a known symbol.
- Added `PlanExpressionKind::FileWriteBytes`.
- MachinePlan lowering emits ordered operands as:
  - `State(BYTES input)`,
  - `Constant(TEXT safe relative path)`.
- `boon_plan::verify_plan` now includes
  `file-write-bytes-ops-well-formed`, requiring:
  - no source-payload field,
  - no update constant,
  - TEXT output state,
  - exactly one source input,
  - one declared BYTES state input,
  - one safe static TEXT path constant.
- The CPU root-scalar PlanExecutor now executes `FileWriteBytes` by:
  - reading bytes through the private root BYTES sidecar,
  - resolving the path relative to the source/scenario host root,
  - rejecting absolute paths, parent traversal, missing/escaping parents,
    directory targets, and symlink targets,
  - writing a temp file in the same directory with `create_new`,
  - renaming into place,
  - canonicalizing and rereading the target,
  - verifying readback bytes and SHA-256.
- Executor update reports now include an explicit `host_effect` object. The
  public report still does not expose inline bytes.
- Added `cargo xtask verify-bytes-file-write-plan`, which generates its source,
  scenario, output file, and negative reports under `target/generated/`.
- Added a dedicated `verify-bytes-file-write-plan` report-schema branch. It:
  - recompiles the source-derived MachinePlan,
  - verifies plan hash/version/capability summary,
  - requires exactly one `FileWriteBytes` op,
  - validates BYTES input and TEXT output storage,
  - checks the scenario top-level `source` and selected
    `expected_source_event.source`,
  - binds executor source id, update op id, target state, update value, and
    host effect,
  - rereads the output artifact and compares length, digest, first/last byte,
  - checks zero fallback counters,
  - rejects public `inline_bytes`,
  - verifies all negative report artifacts exist, hash correctly, and are
    rejected by the schema.

Fresh generated report:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
  - report SHA-256:
    `98f8a82f7c5fb44e66b0799e839faacb49784eea9c2dd9d6dc7140d0f4f184f6`

    `a5fb64c7107f60b95eab3ed94694e9261855b8839b4ef61ab22c4c7579fca129`

  - `negative_case_count=10`
  - `artifact_sha256s` count `13`
  - `per_step_pass_fail` count `25`
  - output artifact:
    `target/generated/bytes-file-write-plan-fixtures/outputs/payload.bin`
  - output bytes: `deadbeef`
  - output SHA-256:
    `5f78c33274e43fa9de5659265c1d917e25c03722dcb0b8d27db8d5feaa813953`
  - fallback counters:
    - `runtime_ast_eval_count=0`
    - `executable_string_path_count=0`
    - `unknown_plan_op_count=0`
    - `graph_rebuild_count=0`
    - `graph_clones_per_item=0`

Write report negative cases:

- `forged-host-effect-digest`
- `forged-byte-length`
- `forged-last-byte`
- `public-inline-bytes-leak`

- `missing-negative-evidence`

All 10 negative case JSON files are preserved under
`target/generated/bytes-file-write-negative-reports/` and hashed in the main
report.

Independent review reports:

  - report SHA-256:
    `e31b06fd7deeb8f2772a688f2558ae27ae9d7f3e8ae55ff4800fda1eab7f3b23`
  - No critical/high runtime blocker remained.

    `pipe_input_expected_type` had a duplicate branch; argument typing now

  - Medium finding addressed: scenario source and expected write source route
    are now bound.
  - Medium finding deferred: the write tamper matrix is still smaller than the
    mature `File/read_bytes` matrix.

- Satisfied for a narrow static-path typed MachinePlan `File/write_bytes`
  vertical slice.
- Satisfied for generic runtime host-effect execution from private BYTES
  sidecar to a sandboxed host file.
- Satisfied for dedicated report-schema validation of source, scenario, plan,
  executor trace, host effect, output artifact, zero fallback counters, and
  preserved report-level negative artifacts.
- Not satisfied for full Phase 7:
