  - typed tagged result values such as `Ok | WriteError[...]`,
  - live filesystem negative matrix,
  - byte source payloads,
  - measured byte banks,
  - fixed-byte zero-allocation proof,
  - broad no-panic/OOB proof.
- Not satisfied for full Phase 8:
  - final BYTES asset descriptors,
  - Cells formula byte scanning.
- Not satisfied for full Phase 9:
  - aggregate stale/tamper gate coverage for this write slice,
  - release performance measurements,
  - full TodoMVC/Cells PlanExecutor parity.
- No speedup is claimed. No release benchmark was run.

## Phase 3/6/8/9 Follow-up: Cells Formula ASCII BYTES Scanning

Date: `2026-06-19`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Changed files in this follow-up:

- `examples/cells/formula.bn`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/architecture/BYTES_SEMANTICS.md`
- `docs/examples/CELLS_CIRCUIT_STYLE.md`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede4a-0aaf-7652-9f08-33eeabe8e162-cells-ascii-bytes-formula-audit.json`

Implementation:

- `examples/cells/formula.bn` now keeps public formula state as TEXT but
  converts the trimmed formula expression once through
  `Text/to_bytes(encoding: Ascii)` before grammar scanning.
- Operator scans for `+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, comma, colon, and `add(`/`sum(`
  prefixes now use BYTES operations (`Bytes/find`, `Bytes/slice`, and
  `Bytes/starts_with`) while still slicing the original TEXT after the strict
  ASCII boundary succeeds.
- ASCII conversion errors now stop as deterministic `parse_error` before a
  recoverable BYTES error can flow into `Bytes/find` and bind as an index.
- Legacy/runtime BYTES conversion now supports explicit `Ascii` as well as
  `Utf8`:
  - `Text/to_bytes(encoding: Ascii)` rejects non-ASCII TEXT with
    `bytes_encode_error_ascii`.
  - `Bytes/to_text(encoding: Ascii)` rejects bytes above `0x7F` with
    `bytes_decode_error_ascii`.
- The PlanExecutor/runtime conversion helpers share the same encoding
  normalization, so root-scalar PlanExecutor conversion behavior does not
  diverge from legacy runtime builtins.
- `boon_plan::verify_plan` now accepts typed `Ascii` constants for
  `TextToBytes` and `BytesToText` plan ops while still rejecting unsupported
  encodings such as `Utf16`.
- Older type-inference helper paths now use `builtin_argument_expected_type`
  instead of bypassing `text_argument_expected_type`; this prevents named
  `Text/substring(start:, length:)` arguments from falling through to the broad
  all-TEXT argument rule.
- `docs/architecture/BYTES_SEMANTICS.md` and
  `docs/examples/CELLS_CIRCUIT_STYLE.md` document the strict ASCII grammar
  boundary and the rule that ordinary literal cell text remains TEXT.

Independent review report:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede4a-0aaf-7652-9f08-33eeabe8e162-cells-ascii-bytes-formula-audit.json`
  - report SHA-256:
    `522a144a658326bbe971b28cebef0bf16dfa1f6ad6c2915e21fd886d3367eeaf`
  - High finding addressed: formula state/deltas must remain TEXT while grammar
    scanning uses BYTES only behind an explicit ASCII boundary.
  - High finding addressed: failed ASCII conversion must become `parse_error`
    before BYTES search branches can bind it as a lowercase pattern.
  - Medium finding addressed: older type-inference helper paths bypassed the
    newer builtin argument expectations.
  - Medium finding addressed: MachinePlan verification had a UTF-8-only
    conversion constant policy.
  - Medium finding deferred: full Cells scenario parity is still blocked by the
    pre-existing `commit-c0-formula-bar-sum-through-a3` recomputation failure.

Commands run for this follow-up:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime cells_formula_scans_with_ascii_bytes_after_text_boundary -- --nocapture` | Pass | Source-shape guard confirms formula scanning uses `Text/to_bytes(encoding: Ascii)`, `Bytes/find`, and `Bytes/starts_with`, and no longer uses `Text/find`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime bytes_runtime_builtins -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Initial pass exposed missing ASCII support in the live runtime builtin fixture. After adding ASCII encode/decode support and deterministic error cases, both BYTES runtime tests passed. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime pure_boon_cells_helpers_support_documented_arithmetic_ops -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Initial failures exposed typechecker named-argument overchecks, then an `add(8,2)` slicing regression. After fixing type inference and scanning an `inner_bytes` slice while preserving original TEXT slicing semantics, the test passed with added non-ASCII formula/literal coverage. |
| `cargo test -p boon_typecheck bytes_builtin_signatures_have_boon_types -- --nocapture` | Pass | BYTES builtin typing fixture still passes after text argument expectation changes. |
| `cargo test -p boon_plan ascii_text_bytes_conversion_lowers_to_typed_executable_plan_ops -- --nocapture` | Pass | New regression proves typed MachinePlan conversion ops accept `Ascii` constants. |
| `cargo test -p boon_plan text_bytes_conversion_updates_lower_to_typed_executable_plan_ops -- --nocapture` | Pass | Existing UTF-8 conversion plan test still passes, and unsupported `Utf16` tamper still fails verification. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime pure_boon_cells -- --nocapture` | Pass | Related Cells behavior tests passed: arithmetic/add/sum/non-ASCII literal/formula, stale dependency removal, and fanout recomputation. |
| `cargo run -q -p boon_cli -- run examples/cells.bn --scenario examples/cells.scn --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-ascii-formula-run.json` | Fail | Full Cells scenario still fails at the known baseline failure: `commit-c0-formula-bar-sum-through-a3: recomputed expected ["C0"], got []`. No full Cells parity is claimed. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Fail, then Pass | First run reported formatting drift after code edits. `cargo fmt --all` was run; the final check passed. |
| `cargo check -p boon_plan -p boon_runtime -p boon_typecheck` | Pass | Focused compile passed after runtime/typecheck/plan changes. |
| `git diff --check -- examples/cells/formula.bn crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs docs/architecture/BYTES_SEMANTICS.md docs/examples/CELLS_CIRCUIT_STYLE.md docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md` | Pass | No whitespace errors in the touched source/docs/ledger paths. |

Exit-gate status for this follow-up:

- Satisfied for the focused Phase 8 Cells formula scanner refactor: formulas
  remain TEXT in user-facing state, while grammar search uses BYTES after an
  explicit strict ASCII boundary.
- Satisfied for deterministic non-ASCII behavior:
  - non-ASCII literal text remains ordinary TEXT;
  - non-ASCII formula syntax produces `parse_error`.
- Satisfied for runtime and typed MachinePlan support for `Ascii` conversion
  constants on the covered conversion operations.
- Satisfied for focused Cells runtime behavior around arithmetic operators,
  `add`, `sum`, stale dependency removal, and fanout recomputation.
- Not satisfied for full Phase 8 example parity: the full
  `examples/cells.scn` still fails at
  `commit-c0-formula-bar-sum-through-a3`.
- Not satisfied for full Phase 9: no aggregate gate, stale/tamper matrix, or
  release performance measurement was added for this slice.
- No speedup is claimed. No release benchmark was run.

## Phase 8/9 Follow-up: Cells Recompute Accounting Runtime Fix

Date: `2026-06-19`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Changed files in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-ascii-formula-run.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede5f-0a6d-7da1-a3e4-b78a11d142fe-cells-recompute-accounting-audit.json`

Root cause:

- The full Cells scenario was failing at
  `commit-c0-formula-bar-sum-through-a3` with
  `recomputed expected ["C0"], got []`.
- Two independent explorer audits confirmed that `C0.value` was computed by the
  generic derived evaluator during source-action row followups:
  `SourceAction::IndexedText` commits `formula_text`, then
  `apply_indexed_row_commit_followups` calls
  `recompute_generic_derived_for_row`.
- The later changed-read fanout pass called
  `recompute_generic_derived_after_changes`, which cleared
  `generic_derived_state.last_recomputed` before scenario assertions. Because
  `C0.value` was already current, the later pass did not record it again.
- This was an engine/runtime accounting bug, not a Cells Boon source bug.

Implementation:

- `LoadedRuntime` source-event paths now clear `last_recomputed` once at the
  beginning of the source-event step.
- `recompute_generic_derived_after_changes` remains the reset wrapper for
  standalone callers.
- A new accumulating helper preserves same-step recomputes across:
  - direct source-action row followups;
  - later dirty/fanout recomputes.
- `apply_counted_step` and the normal generic step executor now use the
  accumulating helper after source actions.
- Added focused regression coverage:
  `cells_source_event_recompute_samples_accumulate_source_action_followups`.
  It asserts that a direct formula commit exposes `cells[2].value` in
  `LiveTurnOutput.recomputed_field_samples`.
- No Boon example workaround was used.

Independent review report:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede5f-0a6d-7da1-a3e4-b78a11d142fe-cells-recompute-accounting-audit.json`
  - report SHA-256:
    `e6b9d8e33177b578951521f0a557d7120f6d5966a861683d35667a2ea238f52e`
  - High finding addressed: same-row source-action derived recomputes were real
    but were erased from `last_recomputed` by the later changed-read recompute
    phase.
  - High finding addressed: recompute accounting is now step-scoped.
  - Medium finding addressed: focused regression coverage checks step metrics.
  - Medium finding addressed: full `examples/cells.scn` now passes after the
    runtime fix.
  - Medium finding deferred: this does not complete full BYTES/MachinePlan
    parity, default PlanExecutor switch, or release performance proof.

Commands run for this follow-up:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo run -q -p boon_cli -- run examples/cells.bn --scenario examples/cells.scn --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-ascii-formula-run.json` | Fail, then Pass | Initial run reproduced `commit-c0-formula-bar-sum-through-a3: recomputed expected ["C0"], got []`. After the runtime accounting fix, the same scenario command exited 0. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Formatting check passed after runtime/test edits. |
| `cargo test -q -p boon_runtime pure_boon_cells_helpers_support_documented_arithmetic_ops -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | First run failed due an over-specific new assertion reading `last_recomputed` after state-summary generation. The regression was moved to step metrics; the existing helper test then passed. |
| `cargo test -q -p boon_runtime cells_source_event_recompute_samples_accumulate_source_action_followups -- --nocapture` | Pass | New focused regression verifies same-step source-action recompute samples survive the later fanout phase. |
| `cargo test -q -p boon_runtime cells_scenario_runs_and_detects_cycle -- --nocapture` | Pass | Full runtime scenario test passes, including the former C0 recompute assertion and later cycle coverage. |
| `cargo check -p boon_runtime` | Pass | Focused compile passed after the runtime accounting change. |

The subagent report was then validated separately. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede5f-0a6d-7da1-a3e4-b78a11d142fe-cells-recompute-accounting-audit.json` | Fail, then Pass | First run exposed the report's `generated_at_utc` format mismatch. After changing it to Unix seconds, the subagent report validated. Existing `boon_native_gpu` dead-code warnings were emitted while compiling dependencies. |

## Phase 9 Follow-up: Cells Derived-Value Runtime Metadata Classifier

Date: `2026-06-19`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Changed files in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-ascii-formula-run.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede6a-e734-7dc2-a6ae-a944875c4c97-cells-runtime-metadata-audit.json`

Root cause:

- `generic_derived_value_semantic_delta_emitter` was incorrectly tied to
  `compiled.derived_text_transform_count > 0`.
- Cells has `derived_value_count = 8` and `derived_text_transform_count = 0`.
  Its `value` and `error` fields still emit derived-value semantic deltas, so
  the old classifier under-reported runtime capability.

Implementation:

- `generic_derived_value_semantic_delta_emitter` now uses
  `compiled.derived_value_count > 0`.
- `generic_derived_text_transform_executor` remains tied to
  `compiled.derived_text_transform_count > 0`.
- The Cells scenario runtime test now asserts:
  - compiled `derived_value_count > 0`;
  - compiled `derived_text_transform_count == 0`;
  - derived-value semantic delta emitter is `true`;
  - derived-text-transform executor is `false`.
- Report-schema evidence validation now mirrors `derived_value_count` against
  `compiled_schedule`, matching the existing mirror for
  `derived_text_transform_count`.
- The runtime schema tamper test now rejects a drifted
  `generic_runtime_slice_evidence.derived_value_count`.
- Cells report-schema validation now feature-gates append/remove/count-retain
  runtime slices from `generic_runtime_slice_evidence` counts instead of
  requiring examples to claim unsupported list operations.
- The Cells-specific schema list no longer requires stale
  `generic_cells_*` slice names that the runtime does not emit; it checks the
  corresponding generic runtime slices already present in reports.

Independent review report:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede6a-e734-7dc2-a6ae-a944875c4c97-cells-runtime-metadata-audit.json`
  - report SHA-256:
    `0cbeaf2b2623af9f0e1d2b2dd81c69dac9b24f6aaf26be194545c07135d82486`
  - High finding addressed: derived-value semantic delta capability must be
    driven by `derived_value_count`, not text transform count.
  - Medium finding addressed: focused Cells metadata assertions cover the split.
  - Medium finding addressed: schema evidence now mirrors `derived_value_count`.
  - Medium finding addressed: full Cells scenario report schema validation no
    longer requires unsupported list/remove slices or stale Cells-only slice
    aliases.

Commands run for this follow-up:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test -q -p boon_runtime cells_scenario_runs_and_detects_cycle -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | First failures exposed broad list/remove schema requirements and stale `generic_cells_*` required keys. After feature-gating list operation slices and aligning Cells requirements to emitted generic slices, the test passed with full `verify_runtime_execution_metadata` coverage. |
| `cargo test -q -p boon_runtime runtime_execution_schema_rejects_adapter_or_incomplete_generic_slices -- --nocapture` | Pass | Existing schema negative test passes with added `derived_value_count` evidence tamper coverage. |
| `cargo run -q -p boon_cli -- run examples/cells.bn --scenario examples/cells.scn --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-ascii-formula-run.json` | Pass | Regenerated the Cells scenario report after the classifier fix. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-ascii-formula-run.json` | Fail, then Pass | First failures exposed `generic_derived_value_semantic_delta_emitter=false`, then list/remove slices required for Cells, then stale `generic_cells_*` required keys. After the classifier and schema fixes, the Cells scenario report validates. Existing `boon_native_gpu` dead-code warnings were emitted while compiling dependencies. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede6a-e734-7dc2-a6ae-a944875c4c97-cells-runtime-metadata-audit.json` | Fail, then Pass | First run rejected a pass report with a failing per-step item. After updating the report to record the schema fix as addressed, the report validated. Existing `boon_native_gpu` dead-code warnings were emitted while compiling dependencies. |
| `cargo check -p boon_runtime -p boon_report_schema` | Pass | Focused compile passed after classifier and schema-evidence changes. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Fail, then Pass | First run found formatting drift in a new assertion; after `cargo fmt --all`, the final check passed. |
| `git diff --check -- crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md` | Pass | No whitespace errors in the touched tracked paths. |

Report artifacts:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-ascii-formula-run.json`
  - SHA-256:
    `e22bf9838748714f7540cd51cc36152bbc5a5bc83906a0ff36a700d8beddd637`
  - Current key evidence:
    - `derived_value_count = 8`
    - `derived_text_transform_count = 0`
    - `generic_derived_value_semantic_delta_emitter = true`
    - `generic_derived_text_transform_executor = false`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede6a-e734-7dc2-a6ae-a944875c4c97-cells-runtime-metadata-audit.json`
  - SHA-256:
    `0cbeaf2b2623af9f0e1d2b2dd81c69dac9b24f6aaf26be194545c07135d82486`

Exit-gate status for this follow-up:

- Satisfied for the focused derived-value runtime metadata classifier bug.
- Satisfied for schema evidence mirroring of `derived_value_count`.
- Satisfied for full Cells scenario report schema validation for the refreshed
  report path in this slice.
- Not satisfied for full Phase 9:
  - no aggregate BYTES/MachinePlan gate was run;
  - no release benchmark was run;
  - no stale/tamper matrix was expanded beyond this evidence mirror and the
    existing runtime metadata negative tests.
- No speedup is claimed.

## Phase 9 Follow-up: BYTES/MachinePlan Aggregate Check-Existing Gate

Date: `2026-06-19`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Changed files in this follow-up:

- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all-missing-check-existing.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/build-bytes-boundary.json`
- refreshed curated `target/reports/bytes-plan/*dump-plan.json`,
  `*run-plan.json`, route, TodoMVC, and Cells report artifacts
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede9a-85f0-7fb1-af6f-88526e5ca73a-bytes-machine-plan-all-adversarial-audit.json`

Implementation:

- Added `cargo xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing`.
- The aggregate validates the curated current BYTES/MachinePlan evidence set
  without rerunning child gates:
  - required report presence;
  - child status;
  - exact command;
  - exact measurement mode;
  - current commit;
  - child report-schema validity;
  - child artifact hash freshness;
  - child report file SHA-256 binding in the aggregate `artifact_sha256s`;
  - zero PlanExecutor fallback counters for reports that expose
    `plan_executor`.
- The aggregate fails without `--check-existing` and writes a schema-valid
  blocker report.
- Report schema now has a dedicated pass verifier for
  `verify-bytes-machine-plan-all`.
- Report schema also accepts failing blocker reports for this command so
  missing/stale evidence produces valid failure evidence.
- The schema initially accepted self-consistent but forged aggregate summaries.
  After independent review, it now enforces the exact 30 child-report contract:
  label, path, command, measurement mode, count, aggregate git commit, and
  child SHA binding.
- Report-schema replay for `run-plan-root-scalar-scenario` now includes
  `host_effect: null` for non-host root-scalar update records, matching the
  runtime report shape.

Independent review:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede9a-85f0-7fb1-af6f-88526e5ca73a-bytes-machine-plan-all-adversarial-audit.json`
  - report SHA-256:
    `2230e3e111592a4a3a41726feee3e97287c240238afcff5ad0c3da708aae7aca`
  - High finding addressed: schema could accept a forged aggregate with a
    reduced child report set and adjusted counts.
  - High finding addressed: schema did not enforce exact child command, git,
    label, or measurement mode.
  - Confirmed behavior: producer requires `--check-existing` and checks child
    presence/status/command/measurement/git/schema/artifact freshness.
  - Confirmed behavior after follow-up: mutation probes reject reduced child
    set, edited command/git/label/mode, forged child SHA, and forged artifact
    SHA.

Commands run for this follow-up:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Fail, then Pass | First run exposed formatting drift after aggregate/schema edits. After `cargo fmt --all`, later checks passed. |
| `cargo check -p xtask -p boon_report_schema` | Pass | Focused compile passed; existing `boon_native_gpu` dead-code warnings were emitted. |
| `cargo xtask help | rg 'verify-bytes-machine-plan-all'` | Pass | Confirmed command registration. Existing native GPU dead-code warnings were emitted while compiling dependencies. |
| `cargo xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all-missing-check-existing.json` | Expected Fail | Missing `--check-existing` produced a blocker report. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all-missing-check-existing.json` | Pass | The missing-flag blocker report is schema-valid. |
| `cargo xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Interrupted, then Fail, then Pass | First run showed stale `boon_cli` child report hashes and later stale/failing BYTES op scenario reports. After refreshing child reports with current binaries and `--compare-legacy`, the aggregate passed. |
| `target/debug/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Pass | Final aggregate report validates. |
| `target/debug/xtask verify-report-schema target/generated/bytes-machine-plan-all-negative-reports/*.json` via mutation loop | Pass as negative matrix | All tested mutations were rejected: missing child, reduced child set with adjusted counts, edited child status, edited `schema_valid`, edited command, edited git, edited label, edited mode, forged child SHA, forged artifact SHA, checked count mismatch, and `aggregate_checks_pass=false`. |
| `cargo build -p boon_cli` | Pass | Rebuilt `boon_cli` before refreshing binary-bound CLI report evidence. |
| `target/debug/boon_cli dump-plan ...` for curated BYTES/TodoMVC/Cells reports | Pass | Refreshed binary-bound dump-plan reports. |

| `target/debug/boon_cli run-plan-route ...` for TodoMVC text/filter routes | Pass | Refreshed source route parity reports. |
| `target/debug/boon_cli run-plan-root-scalar-scenario ...` for TodoMVC submit/toggle/delete/root-scalar slices | Pass | Refreshed curated TodoMVC plan reports. |
| `target/debug/boon_cli run examples/cells.bn --scenario examples/cells.scn --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-ascii-formula-run.json` | Pass | Refreshed full Cells semantic report. |
| `target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-negative --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json` | Fail, then Pass | First failure exposed a schema replay mismatch for `host_effect: null` in root-scalar update records. After fixing the schema replay, the gate passed. |
| `target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-file-read-plan --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json` | Pass | Refreshed File/read_bytes proof under final xtask binary. |
| `target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-file-write-plan --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json` | Pass | Refreshed File/write_bytes proof under final xtask binary. |
| `target/debug/xtask verify-build-bytes-boundary --report target/reports/bytes-plan/build-bytes-boundary.json` | Pass | Refreshed build asset BYTES boundary proof under final xtask binary. |
| `target/debug/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ede9a-85f0-7fb1-af6f-88526e5ca73a-bytes-machine-plan-all-adversarial-audit.json` | Pass | Independent review artifact validates. |

    surfaces;

## Phase 9 Follow-up: TodoMVC Release Benchmark Report and Measurement Summary

Date: `2026-06-19`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Changed files in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-release-benchmark-speed.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-release-benchmark.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edeb4-00d5-7442-8af2-d1591971b5c1-release-benchmark-audit.json`

Implementation:

- `run_scenario` now records `load_pipeline_profile` in runtime reports:
  parse time, lower time, detailed lower profile, verification time, expression
  count, graph node count, source unit count, and total load time.
- `run_loaded_scenario` now records `compile_pipeline_profile`: typed runtime
  compile time, runtime initialization time, expression count, graph node count,
  and total compile/init time.
- Speed reports now include `runtime_step_profile_ms_p50_p95_p99_max`, derived
  from the existing per-step `runtime_step_profile` data.
- Speed reports now include explicit `copy_counters.status = unavailable`
  instead of pretending copy counts are measured. Current available copy-cost
  proxies are heap allocation counters and BYTES digest/length evidence.
- `bench-todomvc` / `bench-example` now omit `stress_profiles` when the linked
  software-dynamic speed report omits the field. This fixes the old benchmark
  wrapper schema failure where the wrapper wrote `stress_profiles: null`.
- Benchmark reports now include `release_measurement_summary` and
  `phase0_baseline_comparison`.
- Phase 0 baseline speed reports are shape-checked and hash-bound as baseline
  artifacts instead of being rejected for stale binary hashes. That matters
  because an old baseline report should not have today's `target/release/xtask`
  binary hash.
- Report schema now requires benchmark reports to include
  `release_measurement_summary`, positive percentile sample counts for the
  main latency summaries, `phase0_baseline_comparison`, and
  `speedup_claim = not_claimed`.

Independent review:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edeb4-00d5-7442-8af2-d1591971b5c1-release-benchmark-audit.json`
  - report SHA-256:
    `309ae300377222e0944571206b3bdd851d101e0693b200275e3dbc6cbe2f475a`
  - High finding addressed: old benchmark wrapper copied `stress_profiles`
    incorrectly for software-dynamic speed reports.
  - Medium finding addressed: release benchmark evidence did not surface
    compile/lower/runtime-init timing or tick-internal summaries.
  - Medium finding addressed: old baseline speed reports were incorrectly
    validated against the current binary hash instead of treated as baseline
    artifacts.
  - Medium finding deferred: Cells release benchmark and broader repeated
    BYTES/MachinePlan performance coverage remain open.

Commands run for this follow-up:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Fail, then Pass | Initial failures were formatting drift after the runtime/schema/xtask edits. `cargo fmt --all` was run and the final check passed. |
| `cargo check -p xtask -p boon_runtime -p boon_report_schema` | Pass | Focused compile passed; existing `boon_native_gpu` dead-code warnings were emitted. |
| `target/debug/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-bench-speed.json` | Pass | Confirmed the old Phase 0 TodoMVC speed report remains schema-valid before using it as a baseline artifact. |
| `cargo xtask bench-todomvc --iterations 20 --report target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-release-benchmark.json --speed-report target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-release-benchmark-speed.json --baseline-speed-report target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-bench-speed.json` | Fail, then Pass | First run generated the current speed report but failed because the old baseline speed report had a stale binary hash. After changing baseline handling to shape-check and hash-bind the baseline artifact, the rerun passed through release `xtask`. |
| `target/release/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-release-benchmark-speed.json target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-release-benchmark.json` | Pass | Current release speed and benchmark reports validate. |
| `jq 'del(.release_measurement_summary)' target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-release-benchmark.json \| target/release/xtask verify-report-schema /dev/stdin` | Expected Fail | Negative probe rejected a benchmark report without `release_measurement_summary`. The wrapper command returned success only after confirming rejection. |
| `target/release/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edeb4-00d5-7442-8af2-d1591971b5c1-release-benchmark-audit.json` | Pass | Performance/storage review artifact validates. |

Report artifacts:

  - SHA-256:
    `6edd3072518d211c80162ed792fdad3fa88406c26fa9896610d34cf10a571a2d`

  per-step tick-internal summaries in runtime speed reports.
- Not satisfied for full Phase 9:
  - Cells release benchmark evidence was not produced in this slice;
  - broader BYTES/MachinePlan-specific performance coverage remains open;
  - repeated benchmark reproduction remains open;
  - copy counters remain explicitly unavailable instead of measured;
  - full workspace checks were not rerun in this slice.
- No speedup is claimed.

### Cells TASK-0804A Follow-up: Rejected Same-row Skip and Current Hot Path

Date: `2026-06-19`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Changed files in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark-speed.json`

Status: unfinished / postponed for a later focused scheduler fix.

What is now proven:

- The earlier RowRef / single-row `selected_input` model is still correct:
  `selected_input` remains a one-row projection and no longer copies a full
  row object for every selector update.
- The remaining release-benchmark blocker is not graph rebuilds or row scans:
  graph rebuilds are `0`, rows scanned p95/max are `0 / 0`, and rows touched
  p95/max are `1 / 1`.
- The current hot path is repeated root list-view work for
  `store.selected_input`. In the slowest step, `cycle-error`, the speed report
  shows two `store.selected_input` samples:
  - changed sample: `elapsed_ms = 1.137281`,
    `eval_ms = 1.035362`, `row_materialize_ms = 0`,
    `list_storage_mode = row_ref_projection_reuse`,
    `current_dirty_read_count = 10`,
    `current_dirty_list_column_read_count = 5`,
    `current_dirty_list_field_read_count = 5`;
  - unchanged sample: `elapsed_ms = 1.142151`,
    `eval_ms = 1.096420`, `row_materialize_ms = 0`,
    `list_storage_mode = row_ref_projection_reuse`,
    `current_dirty_read_count = 4`,
    `current_dirty_list_column_read_count = 2`,
    `current_dirty_list_field_read_count = 2`.
- That means the fixed row representation removed row materialization cost, but
  scheduler/root-list-view evaluation still re-evaluates the same semantic
  projection before and after row-derived followups.

Rejected experiment:

- A speculative same-row dedupe tried to remember source-action followup reads
  and recomputed keys, then remove those keys from the later generic derived
  dirty set.
- The experiment was rejected because it broke required formula propagation.
  The release benchmark failed correctness with:

```text
commit-c0-formula-bar-sum-through-a3: recomputed expected ["C0"], got []
```

- The reverted shortcut must not be reintroduced as a dirty-key suppression.
  Downstream formula recomputation can depend on the same row-derived value even
  when that row was already recomputed during source-action followups.
- A metric-only piece was retained: source-action row-derived recompute keys
  are now merged back into step-local recompute reporting so scenario checks and
  `LiveTurnOutput.recomputed_field_samples` keep seeing same-row recomputes.

Current speed evidence:

- Report path:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark-speed.json`
- Report SHA-256:
  `86e7a1f301ec17a734dc60f9f55353a29cf700427080f9abfeb07e95d9136fb8`
- Benchmark command that produced the current speed report:

```bash
cargo xtask bench-example cells --iterations 20 \
  --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark.json \
  --speed-report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark-speed.json
```

- Status: fail at benchmark budget / wrapper level. The speed report was
  written, but `latency_p95_budget` and `latency_max_budget` fail.
- Interaction/runtime tick latency:
  - p50: `2.196327 ms`
  - p95: `8.823123 ms`
  - p99/max: `9.580329 ms`
  - sample count: `25`
- Budget failures:
  - allowed p95 input-to-idle: `4.0 ms`
  - measured p95 input-to-idle: `8.823123 ms`
  - allowed max single step: `8.0 ms`
  - measured max single step: `9.580329 ms`
- Tick-internal p95/max:
  - `source_action_eval_ms`: `4.8242709999999995 / 5.527524`
  - `derived_recompute_ms`: `3.479479 / 4.091824`
  - `root_materialization_ms`: `1.179086 / 1.4065029999999998`
  - `source_action_root_materialization_ms`: `1.375819 / 1.705413`
- Allocation counters:
  - bounded-profile allocs after warm-up: `36755`
  - rows scanned p95/max: `0 / 0`
  - rows touched p95/max: `1 / 1`
  - dirty keys p95/max: `9 / 9`
  - recomputed fields p95/max: `5 / 5`
  - recompute candidates p95/max: `6 / 6`

Commands run while restoring correctness after the rejected experiment:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Fail, then Pass | Initial failure was a formatting wrap after code edits. `cargo fmt --all` was run, then the final check passed. |
| `cargo check -p boon_runtime` | Pass | Focused compile passed after removing the rejected skip and adding metric-only recompute accumulation. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime cells_selected_input_list_find_materializes_single_row_storage -- --nocapture` | Pass | Proves `selected_input` remains backed by one projected row after the cleanup. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime cells_source_event_recompute_samples_accumulate_source_action_followups -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Failed when all source-action recompute tracking was removed; passed after retaining metric-only accumulation. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime live_runtime_applies_observed_cells_source_events -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Failed with `commit-b0-formula: recomputed expected ["B0"], got []` until source-action recomputes were merged back into step-local reporting. |
| `rg -n "root_list_view_fresh_reads_by_field\|last_source_action_followed_up_reads\|recompute_generic_derived_after_changes_accumulating_skipping\|cells_indexed_source_actions_do_not_recompute_same_row_derived_twice\|root_list_view_fresh_for_reads\|mark_root_list_view_fresh_for_reads" crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs` | Pass | No rejected shortcut symbols remain. |

Future fix direction:

- Do not suppress row-derived recomputation.
- Coalesce or defer root list-view materialization so a root list view such as
  `store.selected_input` is materialized after row-derived dirty propagation
  reaches a stable point, not both before and after the same source action.
- The correct engine fix should be versioned/currentness-based, probably keyed
  by root path plus dirty read epoch/source-action wave. It must prove that the
  root list-view value is current for all reads that changed in the wave before
  skipping evaluation.
- The scheduler should expose evidence for this directly: per-root
  materialization wave IDs, currentness reason, skipped/merged materialization
  count, and invalidation epoch. A simple `dirty.remove(key)` or unversioned
  fresh-read set is not acceptable.

Exit-gate status:

- Satisfied for restoring focused Cells correctness after the rejected
  experiment.
- Satisfied for documenting the actual current slow path with speed-report
  evidence.
- Not satisfied for TASK-0804A / Cells release performance:
  - p95/max latency still exceed budget;
  - root list-view scheduler coalescing is not implemented;
  - no repeated passing release benchmark exists;
  - no speedup is claimed.

## Phase 7/9 Follow-up: Typed BYTES SOURCE Payload Root Scalar Proof

Date: `2026-06-19`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Changed files in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_cli/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `examples/bytes_source_payload_plan_ops.bn`
- `examples/bytes_source_payload_plan_ops.scn`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-plan-ops-dump-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-route-run-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edf2f-25ae-7763-9b3f-d17ea8bb7707-source-payload-bytes-audit.json`

Status: focused Phase 7 byte source-payload root scalar proof satisfied.
The full BYTES/MachinePlan objective remains partial because aggregate
registration, negative/tamper coverage, broader scoped/indexed byte payloads,
performance work, and default-switch gates are still open.

What changed:

- Added `SourcePayloadField::Bytes` through IR/typecheck/plan/runtime/schema
  plumbing.
- Added `payload_bytes` to generic/live source events and CLI/scenario parsing
  via `bytes_hex` / `--payload-bytes-hex bytes=...`.
- Added typed source-payload byte report support. Source event input artifacts
  retain `inline_bytes` for proof replay; public state summaries still expose
  only BYTES metadata (`$boon_type`, `storage`, `byte_len`, `digest`).
- Added `source_payload_bytes_field_count` to runtime route counts and report
  schema checks so byte payload routes cannot be hidden in generic pointer
  counts.
- Fixed MachinePlan lowering so direct `ReadPath(source.bytes)` and
  `ReadPath(source.text)` payload reads canonicalize to
  `PlanExpressionKind::SourcePayload` with typed `ValueRef::SourcePayload`.
  This fixed the byte private-sidecar loss caused by the older payload-as-
  read-path shape.
- Added `examples/bytes_source_payload_plan_ops.bn` / `.scn`. The scenario
  sends `bytes_hex = "01fe04"` to `store.receive`, stores it as BYTES, then
  uses `Bytes/length` and `Bytes/get(index: 1)` from a later `store.inspect`
  event.
- Added runtime unit coverage:
  `root_scalar_plan_executor_replays_bytes_source_payload_update`.

Evidence:

- Dump report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-plan-ops-dump-plan.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `capability_summary.cpu_plan_executor_complete = true`
  - `capability_summary.cpu_plan_executor_unsupported_op_count = 0`
- Scenario report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `legacy_comparison.passed = true`
  - `legacy_comparison.state_match = true`
  - `legacy_comparison.semantic_delta_match = true`
  - `plan_executor.executed_update_branch_count = 3`
  - `runtime_ast_eval_count = 0`
  - `executable_string_path_count = 0`
  - `unknown_plan_op_count = 0`
  - final `store.received_len = 3`
  - final `store.received_byte = 254`
  - final `store.received.digest =
    2096dcbc716cabc261901f6c15ce242d3bb589284cf8e97ba196710211d7e99a`
- Route report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-route-run-plan.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `source_event.payload_bytes.bytes.byte_len = 3`
  - final `store.received.byte_len = 3`
- Subagent audit:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edf2f-25ae-7763-9b3f-d17ea8bb7707-source-payload-bytes-audit.json`
  - independent auditor reported focused compile pass;
  - independent auditor reported route and scenario pass;
  - open findings are recorded below.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo check -p boon_runtime -p boon_plan -p boon_report_schema -p boon_cli --quiet` | Fail, then Pass | Initial failures were missing byte payload counts/artifact handling and a misplaced `payload_bytes` argument. Passed after the source-payload byte plumbing fixes. |
| `cargo run -p boon_cli -- dump-plan examples/bytes_source_payload_plan_ops.bn --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-plan-ops-dump-plan.json` | Pass | Dump report now shows `cpu_plan_executor_complete=true`, unsupported op count `0`, and receive update `expression_kind=source_payload`, `source_payload_field=Bytes`. |
| `cargo run -p boon_cli -- run-plan-root-scalar-scenario examples/bytes_source_payload_plan_ops.bn --scenario examples/bytes_source_payload_plan_ops.scn --steps receive-bytes,inspect-bytes --compare-legacy --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json` | Fail, then Pass | Initial failure exposed payload-as-`read_path` lowering losing private byte storage. Passed after canonicalizing direct payload reads to `SourcePayload`. |
| `cargo run -p boon_cli -- run-plan-route examples/bytes_source_payload_plan_ops.bn --source store.receive --target-state store.received --payload-bytes-hex bytes=01fe04 --compare-legacy --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-route-run-plan.json` | Pass | Direct route proof for source byte payload storage. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-plan-ops-dump-plan.json target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json` | Pass | Existing `boon_native_gpu` dead-code warnings emitted during xtask compile. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-route-run-plan.json` | Pass | Existing `boon_native_gpu` dead-code warnings emitted during xtask compile. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edf2f-25ae-7763-9b3f-d17ea8bb7707-source-payload-bytes-audit.json` | Fail, then Pass | Initial manually saved audit lacked common report envelope fields. Passed after adding the schema-required report envelope. |
| `cargo test -p boon_plan -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | One verifier test still expected payload `ReadPath`; updated it to tamper a `SourcePayload` op. Final result: 30 passed. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime root_scalar_plan_executor_replays_typed_root_expression_kinds -- --nocapture` | Fail, then Pass | Test still expected text payload copy as `read_path`; updated it to require `source_payload`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime root_scalar_plan_executor_replays_bytes_length_update -- --nocapture` | Pass | Existing byte operation fixture still passes. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime root_scalar_plan_executor_replays_bytes_source_payload_update -- --nocapture` | Pass | New focused byte source-payload fixture passes. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Fail, then Pass | Formatting drift from this slice was fixed with `cargo fmt --all`. |
| `cargo check -p boon_plan -p boon_runtime -p boon_report_schema -p boon_cli -p xtask --quiet` | Pass | Existing `boon_native_gpu` dead-code warnings emitted. |

Open findings from this follow-up:

- Medium: the byte source-payload fixture is not yet registered in
  `verify-bytes-machine-plan-all`; the aggregate still has the previous child
  report set.
- Medium: add a dedicated BYTES source-payload negative/tamper matrix:
  missing `payload_bytes.bytes`, bad digest/length, wrong output type,
  fixed-length mismatch, forged `source_payload_field`, stale report hashes,
  and inline byte leakage outside approved proof input artifacts.
- Medium: runtime guards on `SourcePayloadField::Bytes` currently never match.
  A later slice should either implement typed BYTES guard semantics or reject
  byte guards explicitly during compiler/plan verification.
- Medium: named byte payload keys are not a coherent typed source-payload
  feature. CLI/schema can parse `<field>_bytes_hex`, but typed runtime lookup
  currently supports only the built-in `bytes` payload field. V1 should either
  reject named byte payloads or introduce a real typed named-byte field model.
- Medium: source event proof reports include inline input bytes. This is
  acceptable for the small proof fixture but needs size limits or artifact
  indirection before large source payloads.

Exit-gate status:

- Satisfied for focused root-scalar BYTES source payload parsing, typed
  lowering, PlanExecutor execution, legacy parity, semantic delta parity,
  report schema validation, and independent audit evidence.
- Not satisfied for the full Phase 7 / Phase 9 objective:
  aggregate registration and adversarial/tamper coverage remain open;
  scoped/indexed BYTES payloads are not proven;
  BYTES guards and named byte payload policy remain unresolved;
  performance/default-switch work remains open.

### Follow-up: BYTES source-payload aggregate registration and row-initial storage typing

Date: 2026-06-19
Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

- `crates/boon_plan/Cargo.toml`

Status: focused aggregate registration and Cells row-initial storage typing are
satisfied. The full BYTES/MachinePlan objective remains partial.

What changed:

- Registered the BYTES source-payload dump, route, and scenario child reports
  in `verify-bytes-machine-plan-all` and in report-schema aggregate
  expectations.
- Fixed a real MachinePlan typing bug exposed by `examples/cells.bn`:
  row-initial state slots such as `cell.editing_text` and
  `cell.formula_text` kept `InitialValueKind::RowInitialField` but also leaked
  `PlanValueType::RowInitialField` into executable storage. The verifier then
  correctly rejected `SourcePayload(Text)` updates into non-text storage.
- Added conservative row-initial field value-type inference in `boon_plan`.
  The storage layout and `ValueIndex` now resolve row-initial state value types
  from typecheck expression facts, record-literal list field values, and a
  small plan-local builtin/function-result fallback. Ambiguous or unsupported
  cases stay abstract and continue to fail verification rather than guessing.
- Kept `initial_value_kind = row_initial_field` for explainability while
  setting executable storage `value_type = text` for the proven Cells fields.
- Added `cells_row_initial_fields_get_concrete_storage_types` regression
  coverage.

### Follow-up: BYTES source-payload V1 guard and named-payload policy

Date: 2026-06-19
Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_cli/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edfac-a466-7822-af8b-ec6f2448dfa7-bytes-negative-case-id-schema-audit.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edf94-99c2-7c82-95cc-60dffb18d951-bytes-source-payload-policy-followup-audit.json`
- refreshed stale required `target/reports/bytes-plan/*.json` child reports
  whose recorded binary hash no longer matched the rebuilt `boon_cli` or
  `xtask`

Status: focused V1 BYTES source-payload guard and named-payload policy is
satisfied for compiler, plan, runtime, host/scenario/report parsing, direct
Rust route APIs, negative reports, and aggregate report-schema evidence. The
full BYTES/MachinePlan objective remains partial.

Decisions:

- V1 reserves exactly one BYTES source payload key: `bytes`.
- Named payload fields remain TEXT until a typed named-byte payload model is
  designed. Host boundaries must not silently accept `blob`, `blob_bytes_hex`,
  or other named byte sidecars as BYTES.
- BYTES source payload guards are rejected in V1. The current guard model uses
  string choices, while BYTES payloads are byte artifacts with hashes/lengths;
  accepting them would create misleading comparison semantics.
- Ambiguous BYTES guard or payload typing is a compiler/runtime/report error,
  not a requirement for Boon users to add manual type annotations.

What changed:

- CLI `--payload-bytes-hex` now accepts only `bytes=...`; any other key is a
  V1 error.
- Scenario and report replay parsing accepts only `bytes_hex`; any
  `*_bytes_hex` named key is rejected.
- Direct `LiveSourceEvent.payload_bytes` APIs now validate payload byte keys:
  `run_plan_source_route` rejects named keys before execution/reporting, and
  live source batches validate every event before normalization.
- Typecheck rejects `WHEN` guards over `.bytes` source payloads with a targeted
  diagnostic.
- Plan verification and runtime/report-schema guard matching reject
  `SourcePayloadField::Bytes` guards as a backstop.
- `verify-bytes-negative` now includes:
  - `plan-bytes-source-payload-guard-rejected`
  - `source-payload-named-bytes-scenario-key`
  - `source-payload-named-bytes-direct-api-key`
- Added focused unit coverage:
  - `generic_source_event_rejects_named_bytes_payload_keys_in_v1`
  - `run_plan_route_rejects_named_bytes_payload_keys_in_v1`
  - `rejects_bytes_source_payload_when_guard`
  - `verify_plan_rejects_bytes_source_payload_guards_in_v1`

Evidence:

- Negative report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `negative_case_count = 32`
  - `source-payload-named-bytes-scenario-key`: `pass = true`
  - `source-payload-named-bytes-direct-api-key`: `pass = true`
  - `plan-bytes-source-payload-guard-rejected`: `pass = true`
  - `source-payload-bytes-to-text-output`: `pass = true`
  - `source-payload-fixed-length-mismatch`: `pass = true`
- Aggregate report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `aggregate_checks_pass = true`
  - `required_report_count = 33`
  - `checked_report_count = 33`
  - `proof_report_count = 27`
  - `diagnostic_report_count = 6`
  - `plan_executor_report_count = 24`
  - `no_fallback_plan_executor_count = 24`
  - `blockers = 0`
- Subagent report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edfac-a466-7822-af8b-ec6f2448dfa7-bytes-negative-case-id-schema-audit.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - no medium/high findings
  - accepted low informational note: the aggregate does not inline
    `required_negative_cases`; it schema-validates and hashes the
    `bytes-negative` child report instead

Subagent findings:

- The independent exact-ID auditor confirmed `verify-bytes-negative` emits
  `required_negative_cases`.
- The auditor confirmed `boon_report_schema` enforces a hardcoded 32-ID
  contract over `required_negative_cases`, `negative_cases`, and
  `per_step_pass_fail`.
- The auditor ran read-only mutation probes and confirmed missing required IDs,
  truncation, duplicate IDs, wrong count, `rejected = false`, and failed
  per-step evidence are rejected.
- No medium/high issues remain for this slice.
- File-write report refreshed after the runtime rebuild:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `live_negative_case_count = 11`

  - medium direct-API named byte sidecar finding recorded as resolved

Subagent findings:

- The independent follow-up auditor found no high or critical issues in the
  CLI/scenario/report/typechecker/plan/runtime guard policy path.
- The auditor found one medium gap: direct in-process `LiveSourceEvent` callers
  could still pass named byte sidecar keys before execution. This follow-up

  both a unit test and a durable negative report case.

### Follow-up: Exact BYTES negative case-ID schema enforcement

Date: 2026-06-19
Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

Status: the medium finding for exact `verify-bytes-negative` case-ID
enforcement is resolved.

What changed:

- `verify-bytes-negative` now writes `required_negative_cases` into
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`.
- `boon_report_schema` now has a command-specific
  `verify-bytes-negative` branch.
- The schema branch hardcodes the 32 required BYTES negative case IDs and
  rejects reports when:
  - `negative_case_count` is missing or wrong
  - `required_negative_cases` is missing or not exactly the expected ID list
  - `negative_cases` is missing, truncated, duplicated, reordered, or contains
    an unexpected ID
  - any negative case lacks `category`, `detail`, or `rejected = true`
  - `per_step_pass_fail` is missing, truncated, duplicated, reordered, contains
    an unexpected ID, or has a non-passing row
- Added unit test
  `bytes_negative_schema_requires_exact_case_ids`, which proves the schema
  accepts the complete contract and rejects missing required IDs, truncation,
  a forged pass flag, and duplicate case IDs.

Evidence:

- Negative report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `negative_case_count = 32`
  - `required_negative_cases` length = `32`
- Aggregate report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `aggregate_checks_pass = true`
  - `required_report_count = 33`
  - `checked_report_count = 33`
  - `proof_report_count = 27`
  - `diagnostic_report_count = 6`
  - `plan_executor_report_count = 24`
  - `no_fallback_plan_executor_count = 24`
  - `blockers = 0`

| `cargo xtask verify-bytes-negative --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json` | Pass | Regenerated negative report with 32 `negative_cases` and 32 `required_negative_cases`. |
| `target/debug/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json` | Pass | Focused schema validation with the new exact-ID contract. |
| `cargo xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Fail, then Pass | First run after schema rebuild failed on stale `bytes-file-write-plan.json`; passed after refreshing that child report. |

### Follow-up: Direct plan-verifier source-payload field tamper coverage

Date: 2026-06-19
Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edfb2-65a2-7b92-974d-19a9ece83a93-plan-source-payload-field-tamper-audit.json`

Status: the medium finding for direct plan-verifier/unit coverage of forged
`source_payload_field` mutations is resolved.

What changed:

- Added `verify_plan_rejects_tampered_source_payload_field_after_lowering`.
  This compiles the BYTES source-payload fixture, mutates the lowered update
  branch metadata from `source_payload_field = Bytes` to `Text`, and proves the
  verifier rejects the forged plan.
- Added `verify_plan_rejects_tampered_text_source_payload_field_after_lowering`.
  This compiles the root scalar TEXT source-payload fixture, mutates the
  lowered update branch metadata from `source_payload_field = Text` to `Key`,
  and leaves the output storage as TEXT. This narrower case proves the verifier
  checks the metadata against the typed `ValueRef::SourcePayload(Text)` operand,
  not only against output storage type.

Evidence:

- Focused plan tests:
  - `verify_plan_rejects_tampered_source_payload_field_after_lowering`: pass
  - `verify_plan_rejects_tampered_text_source_payload_field_after_lowering`:
    pass
- Aggregate report remained clean:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `aggregate_checks_pass = true`
  - `required_report_count = 33`
  - `checked_report_count = 33`
  - `blockers = 0`
- BYTES negative report remained clean:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `negative_case_count = 32`
  - `required_negative_cases` length = `32`
- Subagent report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edfb2-65a2-7b92-974d-19a9ece83a93-plan-source-payload-field-tamper-audit.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - no medium/high findings
  - accepted low note: `crates/boon_plan/` is still untracked until the active
    BYTES/MachinePlan work is eventually staged/committed

Subagent findings:

- The independent auditor confirmed the BYTES tamper case rejects
  `Bytes -> Text` metadata mutation through both
  `constant-refs-resolve-and-match-storage-types` and
  `capability-summary-derived-counts`.
- The auditor requested and then confirmed the narrower TEXT route case:
  `Text -> Key` metadata mutation leaves output storage compatible, keeps
  `constant-refs-resolve-and-match-storage-types` passing, and fails
  `capability-summary-derived-counts` through typed payload operand/executor
  support.
- No medium/high issues remain for this slice.

| `cargo check -p boon_plan --quiet` | Pass | Plan crate compiles outside the unit test run. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Formatting clean. |
| `target/debug/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edfb2-65a2-7b92-974d-19a9ece83a93-plan-source-payload-field-tamper-audit.json` | Pass | New subagent audit report validates. |

### Follow-up: Row-initial bool SourcePayload(Text) negative coverage

Date: 2026-06-19
Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`

Status: the medium finding for row-initial over-inference negative coverage is
resolved for the BYTES negative/report-schema contract.

What changed:

- Added exact negative case
  `plan-row-initial-bool-rejects-source-payload-text` to
  `verify-bytes-negative`.
- Added the same case ID to the exact BYTES negative report-schema contract,
  increasing the required negative case count from `32` to `33`.
- Strengthened the fixture after subagent review. The first fixture rejected
  through a weak `row_initial_field` shape, which did not prove the bool field
  invariant. The current fixture uses a multiline `LIST` with both
  `[flag: False]` and `[flag: True]`, then attempts to update that bool
  row-initial field with `edit.text`.
- The expected rejection now binds to the typechecker diagnostic:
  `` `HOLD` update must match the held value type; expected: BOOL; found: TEXT ``.

Evidence:

- BYTES negative report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `negative_case_count = 33`
  - `required_negative_cases` length = `33`
  - `plan-row-initial-bool-rejects-source-payload-text` rejects with
    `expected: BOOL` and `found: TEXT`
- Aggregate report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `aggregate_checks_pass = true`
  - `required_report_count = 33`
  - `checked_report_count = 33`
  - `blockers = 0`
- File-write child report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
  - refreshed after schema validation found its `binary_hash` stale
  - `status = pass`
- Subagent report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edfb8-171c-7410-aa7a-ee3918d11071-row-initial-bool-source-payload-negative-audit.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - medium finding resolved
  - accepted low note: a smaller direct `boon_typecheck` unit may still be
    useful later for faster local regression coverage

Subagent findings:

- The independent auditor found that the first row-initial fixture was too weak:
  it rejected, but because `row.flag` lowered as `row_initial_field`, not
  because the checker had proved a concrete BOOL field.
- The auditor recommended the current two-row bool fixture and exact
  `expected: BOOL` / `found: TEXT` diagnostic.
- After the patch and regenerated reports, no medium/high issues remain for
  this row-initial negative slice.

| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Formatting clean after the source changes. |

| `cargo test -p boon_plan source_payload_field_after_lowering -- --nocapture` | Pass | Previously added direct source-payload tamper tests still pass. |

### Follow-up: Bounded source-event BYTES report artifacts

Date: 2026-06-19
Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this follow-up:

- `crates/boon_cli/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-large-route-run-plan.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edfc7-1c42-76b3-8d3b-39e0f5f6dbf4-source-event-bytes-artifact-audit.json`

Status: the medium finding for large source-event BYTES payload artifact
indirection is resolved for `run-plan-route` reports.

What changed:

- Added a bounded source-event BYTES report contract. `source_event.payload_bytes`
  keeps payload bytes inline only up to `1024` bytes, matching the existing
  MachinePlan byte-constant inline limit.
- Larger source-event BYTES payloads are written as sidecar files and reported
  with `storage = "artifact"`, `byte_len`, `digest`, `artifact_path`,
  `artifact_sha256`, and `inline_byte_limit`.
- The report schema now validates every `source_event.payload_bytes.*` entry,
  rejects public inline payloads over the limit, reads artifact-backed payloads,
  checks digest/length/hash consistency, rejects `inline_bytes` on artifact
  storage, and requires a matching top-level `artifact_sha256s` entry.
- Added `boon_cli run-plan-route --payload-bytes-file bytes=<path>` so large
  CLI route proofs can avoid embedding huge hex payloads in `command_argv`.
- Registered `bytes-source-payload-large-route-run-plan.json` as a required
  `verify-bytes-machine-plan-all` child report.
- Extended `verify-bytes-negative` with large source-payload artifact accept and
  tamper cases.

Evidence:

- Large source-payload route report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-large-route-run-plan.json`
  - `source_event.payload_bytes.bytes.storage = artifact`
  - `byte_len = 1025`
  - `inline_bytes` absent
  - `inline_byte_limit = 1024`
  - `artifact_sha256s` length = `1`
- BYTES negative report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `negative_case_count = 38`
  - `required_negative_cases` length = `38`
  - new large-payload cases:
    - `bytes-source-payload-large-artifact-route-schema-valid`
    - `source-payload-large-missing-artifact-listing`
    - `source-payload-large-forged-artifact-sha256`
    - `source-payload-large-inline-over-limit`
    - `source-payload-large-artifact-inline-leak`
- Aggregate report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `aggregate_checks_pass = true`
  - `required_report_count = 34`
  - `checked_report_count = 34`
  - `blockers = 0`
- Subagent report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edfc7-1c42-76b3-8d3b-39e0f5f6dbf4-source-event-bytes-artifact-audit.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - medium finding resolved
  - accepted low note: `--payload-bytes-hex` remains available for small/debug
    payloads, while `--payload-bytes-file` should be used for large CLI inputs

Subagent findings:

- The independent auditor confirmed that public BYTES values already omitted
  `inline_bytes`; the only report leak was source-event proof input artifacts
  in `source_event.payload_bytes`.
- The auditor recommended using the existing `1024` byte MachinePlan constant
  limit as the source-event inline cap.
- The implemented contract follows that recommendation and adds required
  aggregate evidence plus stale/forged artifact negative cases.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo check -p boon_runtime -p boon_report_schema --quiet` | Pass | Runtime and schema compile after adding artifact-backed source-event reports. |
| `cargo run -p boon_cli -- run-plan-route examples/bytes_source_payload_plan_ops.bn --source store.receive --target-state store.received --payload-bytes-hex bytes=<1025 bytes> --compare-legacy --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-large-route-run-plan.json` | Pass | Initial manual proof that payloads over the cap use `storage = artifact`; later regenerated by xtask. |
| `cargo xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-large-route-run-plan.json` | Pass | Schema accepts the artifact-backed 1025-byte source payload and rejects stale old-schema expectations. |
| `cargo test -p boon_report_schema bytes_negative_schema_requires_exact_case_ids -- --nocapture` | Pass | Exact negative case ID unit accepts the 38-case contract. |
| `cargo xtask verify-bytes-negative --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json` | Pass | Regenerated negative report with 38 required cases, including large artifact accept/reject cases. |
| `cargo xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Fail, then Pass | First run failed only because 29 child reports had stale binary hashes after rebuilding `boon_cli`/`xtask`; after rerunning their recorded `command_argv`, aggregate passed with 34 of 34 reports. |
| `target/debug/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019edfc7-1c42-76b3-8d3b-39e0f5f6dbf4-source-event-bytes-artifact-audit.json` | Pass | New subagent audit report validates. |

Open findings after this follow-up:

- Medium: scoped/indexed BYTES source payloads and performance-oriented payload
  storage remain future work.
- Medium: the default execution switch to PlanExecutor remains blocked by the
  broader Phase 6 through Phase 10 parity, storage, performance, and no-fallback
  gates.
- Low: add a small direct unit around the exact `1024` / `1025` source-event
  report boundary if this path starts changing frequently; current evidence is
  the required aggregate route report plus negative tamper cases.

Exit-gate status:

- Satisfied for bounded `run-plan-route` source-event BYTES payload reports and
  artifact-backed large payload evidence.
- Not satisfied for the full Phase 7 / Phase 9 / Phase 10 objective:
  scoped/indexed BYTES payloads, performance measurement/speedup work, complete

Status: partially resolved. Scoped/indexed BYTES source payload receive/update
now has typed row initial storage, PlanExecutor execution, legacy compare
parity, schema validation, a runtime regression test, and aggregate required
child-report registration. Full row-scoped BYTES consumption remains open
because the follow-up `row.inspect` source does not lower to executable selected
surface work yet.

What changed:

- Added `examples/bytes_indexed_source_payload_plan_ops.bn/.scn` with a
  two-row list, fixed `BYTES[3]` row payload fields, and a row-scoped
  `row.receive.bytes` update for the beta row.
- Fixed row-record literal lowering so `BYTES[3] {}` inside initial row records
  becomes a typed byte constant/row field instead of an enum-like scalar.
- Added `boon_plan` regression coverage proving `row.payload` is an indexed
  `PlanValueType::Bytes { fixed_len: Some(3) }`, the list initial rows contain
  typed byte values, and the update branch uses a typed
  `ValueRef::SourcePayload { field: Bytes }`.
- Added indexed PlanExecutor report evidence for `source_payload_field`, and
  updated report-schema expected replay to require the same indexed payload
  metadata.
- Added a legacy compare path for indexed BYTES source-payload commits using the
  existing generic `eval_bytes` evaluator. This emits a generic `ValueField`
  semantic delta with a public BYTES digest/length summary and no Boon-level
  workaround.
- Registered the indexed BYTES dump-plan and scenario reports as required
  `verify-bytes-machine-plan-all` child reports.

## 2026-06-19 — Indexed BYTES `row.inspect` Consumption Unblocked

Status: resolved for the focused indexed BYTES source-payload readback slice.
The two-step beta-row scenario now executes both `row.receive.bytes` and
`row.inspect` through the CPU PlanExecutor and the legacy comparison path with
typed numeric deltas for `Bytes/length` and `Bytes/get`.

What changed:

- Fixed direct source extraction for row-local bare source identifiers so
  `inspect |> THEN { ... }` is attributed to `row.inspect`, not lost as an
  anonymous row-local expression.
- Extended the legacy source action table with indexed value actions for
  indexed `Bytes/length` and `Bytes/get` derived row fields.
- Added row-private BYTES sidecar handling for PlanExecutor and report-schema
  replay so indexed BYTES payloads are never reconstructed from public digest
  summaries.
- Kept `ListMemory::set_value` strict, but allowed scalar JSON updates into
  existing scalar JSON columns while rejecting structured JSON and scalar JSON
  into text/bool/BYTES columns.
- Corrected NUMBER/BYTE row initializers and row reset paths to materialize as
  scalar JSON, not text.
- Preserved typed numeric semantic deltas by serializing `ProtocolValue::NumberText`
  as a JSON number and by stopping root PlanExecutor semantic deltas from
  stringifying numeric JSON values.
- Added a guarded numeric-from-text fallback for root scalar source actions so
  existing numeric slots do not fall through to the text writer for nested
  numeric `WHEN`/infix source expressions.
- Added a storage regression test proving scalar JSON update behavior and
  explicit indexed report assertions for beta row `payload_len = 3` and
  `payload_second = 254`.

Evidence:

- Root BYTES payload report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-source-payload-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - `legacy_comparison.passed = true`
  - `legacy_comparison.state_match = true`
  - `legacy_comparison.semantic_delta_match = true`
  - `executed_update_branch_count = 3`
  - fallback counters remain zero:
    `runtime_ast_eval_count = 0`, `executable_string_path_count = 0`,
    `unknown_plan_op_count = 0`
- Indexed BYTES payload/readback report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-indexed-source-payload-plan-ops-scenario-run-plan.json`
  - `status = pass`
  - selected steps: `receive-beta-bytes`, `inspect-beta-bytes`
  - `legacy_comparison.passed = true`
  - `legacy_comparison.state_match = true`
  - `legacy_comparison.semantic_delta_match = true`
  - `executed_update_branch_count = 3`
  - `executed_indexed_update_count = 3`
  - beta row report contains `payload_len = 3` and `payload_second = 254`
  - fallback counters remain zero:
    `runtime_ast_eval_count = 0`, `executable_string_path_count = 0`,
    `unknown_plan_op_count = 0`
- Subagent report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee00b-9ea3-7b43-8b44-268b7b564122-indexed-bytes-storage-audit.json`
  - read-only audit confirmed the failure was engine-side in indexed value
    commits and row numeric/text storage, not a Boon source workaround issue.

## 2026-06-19 — Typechecker Contract Fixes for Physical TodoMVC and NovyWave

Status: resolved for the identified engine/typechecker clusters. This does not
complete the broader BYTES/MachinePlan goal: NovyWave still has a separate
runtime stack-overflow failure, aggregate reports still need refresh, and the
default PlanExecutor switch remains blocked by broader parity/performance gates.

Subagent findings:

- NovyWave auditor:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee023-e4ef-7a92-8fce-046e7107a67e-novywave-typecheck-signature-audit.json`
  - The 22-diagnostic NovyWave wall was a typechecker/runtime contract mismatch,
    not a NovyWave source rewrite issue.
  - Runtime already accepts scalar formatting for `Text/concat` and
    `Text/time_range_label`, numeric/boolean options for `Text/to_number` and
    `Number/to_text`, and numeric-text pointer payloads for
    `Number/project_time`.
- Physical TodoMVC auditor:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/019ee023-e653-7f12-b593-2f7579fc6076-physical-todomvc-latest-toggle-audit.json`
  - `LATEST { ... }` was typed as exact empty object `[]` instead of the widened
    non-`SKIP` branch result.
  - `Bool/toggle(when:)` was checked as boolean data, but runtime treats
    `when:` as a tick-present-or-absent pulse.

What changed:

- Added branch-result inference for `AstExprKind::Latest`, including inline
  `LATEST` blocks inside arguments such as
  `List/retain(item, if: LATEST { ... })`.
- Added static `LATEST` result inference for function/shape analysis.
- Changed `Bool/toggle(when:)` checking to validate pulse flow
  (`TickPresent` / `PresentOrAbsent`) while keeping the toggled input itself as
  BOOL.
- Added builtin-local contracts instead of global assignability changes:
  - `Text/to_number(radix:, fallback:)` expects NUMBER.
  - `Text/to_number(leading:)` expects BOOL.
  - `Number/to_text(prefix:)` expects BOOL, while numeric formatting options
    remain NUMBER.
  - `Text/concat` and `Text/time_range_label` accept text-formattable scalar
    values (`TEXT`, `NUMBER`, `BOOL`, or tag-only enum variants).
  - `Number/project_time(pointer_x:, pointer_width:)` accepts NUMBER or numeric
    TEXT, matching current SOURCE payload/runtime behavior.
- Added focused typechecker regressions for inline `LATEST`, source-pulse
  `Bool/toggle(when:)`, rejected continuous `Bool/toggle(when: True)`, and the
  NovyWave-style builtin argument surface.

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_typecheck/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- Subagent audit reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/`

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `cargo test -p boon_typecheck inline_latest_branch_result_satisfies_list_retain_bool_filter --lib -- --nocapture` | Pass | Covers inline `LATEST` branch-result typing inside `List/retain(if:)`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_typecheck bool_toggle_when --lib -- --nocapture` | Pass | Covers accepting source pulses and rejecting continuous `when: True`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_typecheck novywave_style_builtin_argument_shapes_match_runtime_contracts --lib -- --nocapture` | Pass | Covers the narrowed NovyWave builtin signature cluster. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime physical_todomvc_root_source_event_default_materializes -- --nocapture` | Pass | Physical TodoMVC now constructs/runs past the earlier line 2479 `LATEST` typecheck failure; duration `140.19s`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime physical_todomvc_add_todo_defaults_active_and_remains_toggleable -- --nocapture` | Pass | Covers the physical TodoMVC toggle path after `Bool/toggle(when:)` pulse typing; duration `144.74s`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime novywave_initial_bridge_descriptor_uses_initial_format -- --nocapture` | Fail | No longer reports the 22 typecheck diagnostics first; reaches runtime and aborts with stack overflow. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime novywave -- --nocapture` | Fail | Filtered NovyWave run aborts with stack overflow in `novywave_cursor_click_splits_page_identity_from_cursor_freshness`. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime bytes` | Pass | 28 BYTES-filtered runtime tests pass. |
| `cargo test -p boon_runtime root_latest` | Pass | 3 root-latest tests pass. |
| `cargo test -p boon_ir row_local_bare_source_identifier_lowers_indexed_bytes_reads` | Pass | Indexed BYTES IR regression still passes. |
| `cargo test -p boon_report_schema run_plan_root_scalar_scenario_replays_private_bytes_from_concat_across_steps` | Pass | Report-schema private BYTES replay regression still passes. |
| `cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Formatting clean after rustfmt. |

Open findings after this follow-up:

- High: NovyWave still has a runtime recursion/stack-overflow bug. The old
  typecheck wall is addressed, but NovyWave runtime tests cannot be claimed
  passing.
- Medium: physical TodoMVC representative runtime tests pass, but the full
  workspace/runtime suite was not rerun to completion after this fix.
- Medium: the full BYTES/MachinePlan aggregate remains incomplete until stale
  reports are regenerated and broader Phase 6 through Phase 10 gates pass.

Exit-gate status:

  typechecker failures.
