## Phase 9/10 Readiness Refresh and Native TodoMVC Parity Detour

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T21:39:57+02:00`

Status: partial readiness progress. Current BYTES/MachinePlan aggregate evidence
was refreshed for committed `d6eb154`, runtime production hardening and
runtime finality pass for that commit, and the previous native TodoMVC preview
E2E timeout was resolved with a longer run. The full goal remains incomplete:
the current dirty worktree makes the aggregate stale again, machine-readiness
still fails on native TodoMVC reference parity, TASK-0804A remains postponed,
and Phase 10 default execution has not switched.

Base/current commit:

- Base at start of slice: `d6eb154`.
- Current HEAD while recording: `d6eb154`.
- Worktree after this slice is intentionally dirty because the native TodoMVC
  source/renderer fixes are not committed in this goal continuation.

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_document/src/render_scene.rs`
  - `render_text_runs` now honors the public `text_align` style key before the
    older internal `align` key and parses the value case-insensitively.
  - Added `render_text_runs_honor_public_text_align_style`.
- `crates/boon_document/src/lib.rs`
  - `text_align` now participates in font/style identity classification.
- `examples/todomvc.bn`
  - Aligned the initial TodoMVC reference geometry with the native reference:
    removed the title-panel spacer from the active item list, made the
    toggle-all hit area `45`, adjusted the input text inset to keep the text
    gutter aligned, centered the title text, neutralized the unfocused new-row
    border, and changed initial `new_todo_focused` from `True` to `False`.
- `examples/todo_mvc_physical/**`
  - Made the physical theme wrapper delegate `ToggleControl` and
    `TitlePanelGap` to active theme values.
  - Added `NewTodoInputInset` spacing values so Classic can use the same
    45px/15px reference gutter while non-Classic themes retain their existing
    60px/6px geometry.
- `crates/boon_native_playground/src/main.rs`
  - Updated focused TodoMVC geometry tests to the reference 45px Classic
    toggle control and theme-specific physical widths.

Decisions:

- The previous native preview E2E timeout was not treated as proof of a render
  failure. A single longer `900s` rerun produced
  `target/reports/native-gpu/preview-e2e-todomvc.json` with `status=pass`.
- The failing reference parity was not worked around by weakening the verifier.
  The actual causes found were stale source geometry and a renderer bug where
  `text_align: Center` was ignored for stack/container text runs.
- The remaining native reference-parity failure is now narrow: all structural,
  chevron, title-bounds, mean/p95/high-diff pixel checks pass; only the largest
  connected high-diff region remains above the strict threshold.

Follow-up recorded at: `2026-06-21T23:58:00+02:00`

Status: native TodoMVC parity remains incomplete; this detour is intentionally
not considered a BYTES/MachinePlan phase exit.

Additional findings:

- Independent read-only subagent `019eebb4-7ecf-7db0-8265-d7786481e66e`
  confirmed that the dominant connected diff region was the completed todo
  title/strikethrough band (`Finish TodoMVC renderer`) and that the lowered
  item had incorrectly inherited the app/body `line_height: 19.6` while also
  declaring `size: 24`.
- Local component scans confirmed the same region. Before the follow-up fix the
  largest high-diff component was around
  `normalized_crop_largest_mismatch_region_ratio=0.002551020408163265`
  against the `0.002` budget.
- The native document lowering now avoids inheriting an absolute parent
  `line_height` when a child explicitly changes `size` and does not specify its
  own `line_height`.
- The broad TodoMVC lowering test still contained stale geometry assumptions
  from before the reference-aligned source changes. It now expects the title to
  sit directly above the panel and finds the new-todo input at the current
  reference y-coordinate.

Rejected experiments:

- Changing the generic renderer fallback line-height from `1.25 * font_size` to
  `1.2 * font_size` was tested and reverted. It did not improve the accepted
  connected-region metric; the largest connected region increased to
  `0.002653061224489796`.
- Restoring initial `new_todo_focused=True` was tested and reverted. It made
  the native crop draw a red input focus outline that the current title-pixel
  detector counted as red title pixels, causing `large centered red title pixel
  bounds` to fail.

Open follow-up findings:

- The line-height inheritance fix is a real generic lowering fix, but it does
  not complete native TodoMVC reference parity by itself.
- The native reference-parity report currently on disk is stale for the current
  worktree because the failed fallback-line-height experiment was reverted after
  the last comparator run.
- The reference artifact and verifier appear to disagree around the focused
  new-todo input outline: drawing the focus outline makes red title detection
  fail, while not drawing it leaves a visible reference/native input-outline
  mismatch. This should be handled in a future native verifier/reference
  contract slice, not as a BYTES/MachinePlan default-switch blocker workaround.
- Because native parity remains incomplete, the next BYTES/MachinePlan work
  should not claim machine-readiness. It may proceed on independent unfinished
  BYTES/MachinePlan slices with this native detour recorded as unresolved.

## Phase 3/9 Dump-Plan Resolved Constant Report Contract

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T13:23:42+02:00`

Status: partial Phase 3/9 verification evidence; complete for the focused
dump-plan resolved-constant report contract.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_cli/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed aggregate-required dump-plan reports under
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/`
- refreshed stale aggregate child reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`

What changed:

- `boon_cli dump-plan` now emits a narrow typecheck projection:
  `typecheck_report.resolved_constant_table`.
- `verify_machine_plan_report` now requires `typecheck_report` on dump-plan
  diagnostics and compares its resolved-constant projection to a source-derived
  recomputation from the report's `source_files` and `target_profile`.
- The schema check intentionally compares structured source-derived JSON rather
  than fixture names or expected entry counts. This keeps TEXT literals, BYTES
  blobs, paths, and other non-exported values out of the resolved constant
  contract.
- Added schema regression coverage that rejects missing `typecheck_report` and
  tampered `resolved_constant_table.entries`.

## Phase 2/7/9 BYTES Multiline Parser Fixtures And Row BYTES Report Consistency

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T17:35:00+02:00`

Status: partial Phase 2 parser coverage and focused Phase 7/9 report
consistency hardening; complete for multiline `BYTES` constructors with `--`
comments and for the indexed row `row_fields` BYTES summary inconsistency found
while refreshing storage-profile evidence.

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_parser/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Implemented:

- Added a parser-only `merge_multiline_bytes_lines` pass between token lines
  and `ParserItem`s. It merges only unfinished `BYTES ... {` constructor bodies
  across physical lines, preserves the first-line source span, keeps original
  token spans, and skips `TEXT` body line ranges.
- Reused existing `ast_bytes_literal`, `matching_close`, `split_top_level`,
  byte-literal parsing, and existing diagnostics after the merge. No
  typechecker/IR/runtime workaround was added for parser support.
- Added parser coverage for multiline fixed/inferred `BYTES` literals, nested
  multiline `BYTES`, comment-only lines inside a `BYTES` body, trailing
  comments after inline `BYTES`, multiline invalid byte literals, and
  multiline trailing-token rejection.
- Extended `verify-bytes-negative` and report-schema expected IDs with:
  `parser-multiline-invalid-hex-byte` and
  `parser-multiline-trailing-token`.
- Fixed the `bytes_negative_schema_requires_exact_case_ids` synthetic fixture
  so the existing OOB cases include required `panic_free=true` and
  `failure_kind=structured_error` fields.
- Fixed runtime indexed update report rows to overlay authoritative
  `private_bytes` summaries onto emitted `row_fields`. This prevents later row
  updates such as `row.first_byte` from reporting stale public
  `row_fields.file_bytes` summaries after `row.file_bytes` has been updated in
  private BYTES state.

Evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `negative_case_count=52`
  - required parser cases now include
    `parser-invalid-hex-byte`,
    `parser-multiline-invalid-hex-byte`,
    `parser-byte-overflow`,
    `parser-bad-size-expression`,
    `parser-missing-body`, and
    `parser-multiline-trailing-token`.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - case `indexed-file-read-row-field-path-no-copy` now reports
    `borrowed_read_evidence.inspect_updates[0].row_fields.file_bytes.digest`
    as `2096dcbc716cabc261901f6c15ce242d3bb589284cf8e97ba196710211d7e99a`,
    matching the private indexed BYTES state.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `checked_report_count=47`
  - `required_report_count=47`
  - `proof_report_count=40`
  - `diagnostic_report_count=7`
  - `no_fallback_plan_executor_count=33`

| --- | --- | --- |

Final direct run passed with 47/47 reports. |

Independent review:

- Subagent `019ee96f-63e1-7cb3-8b12-84544a11e128` confirmed comments were
  already stripped but true multiline `BYTES` constructors were unsupported
  because `ParserItem` was one physical line and `ast_bytes_literal` required a
  matching body close in that token slice.
- The reviewer recommended a narrow BYTES-only line merge before
  `parser_items`, preserving TEXT body exclusions and reusing existing parser
  diagnostics. The implemented parser fix follows that shape.

Open findings:

- Phase 2 remains partial until a final parser audit proves every parser
  requirement in the objective, including all malformed/unterminated forms, is
  covered at full scope.
- The parser merge is intentionally BYTES-only. If future constructors need
  multiline expression merging, they should get their own audited parser shape
  rather than widening this helper silently.
- The runtime `row_fields` overlay is report/public-snapshot consistency only;
  it does not change PlanExecutor state semantics.

## Phase 7/9 Indexed Private BYTES Summary Tamper Hardening

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T10:37:31+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Status: partial Phase 7 runtime/storage and Phase 9 adversarial evidence;
complete for storage-profile report tampering where indexed row public BYTES
summaries diverge from the private indexed byte-bank evidence.

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`

What changed:

- Storage-profile schema now checks nested row BYTES summaries in the indexed
  host-boundary cases:
  - `indexed-file-write-source-payload-no-copy` must keep
    `updates[0].row_fields.payload` bound to the expected digest/length.
  - `indexed-file-read-row-field-path-no-copy` must keep
    `inspect_updates[0].row_fields.file_bytes` bound to the expected
    digest/length.
- Added aggregate adversarial case
  `aggregate-storage-profile-indexed-private-bytes-tamper-rejected`.
- The tamper mutates
  `borrowed_read_evidence.inspect_updates[0].row_fields.file_bytes.digest` in
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json` while leaving the
  top-level update value, host effect, byte-bank flags, and artifact bytes
  unchanged.
- The aggregate must reject that forged child report through
  `bytes-storage-profile:schema-valid`.

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-adversarial.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `adversarial_case_count=9`
  - `aggregate-storage-profile-indexed-private-bytes-tamper-rejected`
    has `rejected=true`.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `aggregate_checks_pass=true`
  - `required_report_count=47`
  - `checked_report_count=47`
  - `proof_report_count=40`
  - `diagnostic_report_count=7`
  - `plan_executor_report_count=33`
  - `no_fallback_plan_executor_count=33`

## Phase 4/9 MachinePlan Constant Interning Contract

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T11:05:12+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Status: partial Phase 4 semantic/plan representation and Phase 9 verifier
evidence; complete for deduplicating equal `PlanConstantValue`s in the
MachinePlan constant pool and rejecting duplicate constants in `verify_plan`.

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed aggregate reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`

What changed:

- `compile_typed_program` now builds initial constants through the same
  `push_plan_constant` route used by update operands and row expressions.
- `push_plan_constant` now interns by full `PlanConstantValue` equality and
  returns the first matching `PlanConstantId`.
- `append_constant_id` now uses `push_plan_constant`, so list-append row
  literal constants participate in the same pool.
- `verify_plan` now includes the explicit check
  `plan-constants-deduplicated`.
- Existing text/BYTES conversion tests were updated to require
  `Text/to_bytes(encoding: Ascii)` and `Bytes/to_text(encoding: Ascii)` to
  share one interned `Ascii` constant.
- Added focused `boon_plan` coverage proving duplicate BYTES initial literals
  share one `PlanConstantId`, repeated `Bytes/get(index: 0)` update constants
  share one number constant, and a tampered duplicate constant fails
  `plan-constants-deduplicated`.

  warned not to broaden this slice into expression DAG/CSE.

Open findings:

- This closes the duplicate MachinePlan constant-pool gap, including duplicate
  BYTES constant values.
- This does not implement expression DAG sharing, CSE, or row-expression
  structural deduplication. Those remain separate representation/performance
  work.
- Broader Phase 4 source-payload typing summary cleanup, Phase 7 runtime
  storage work, Phase 9 performance coverage, Cells release benchmark evidence,
  and Phase 10 default-switch readiness remain open.

## Phase 7/9 Indexed Row-Field Ownership Hardening

Status: partial Phase 7/9 verifier hardening; complete for the current
mixed-row-shape and fabricated foreign-list row-field dynamic-path cases.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed required reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`

What changed:

- `File/read_bytes` and `File/write_bytes` MachinePlan verification now checks
  that an indexed row-field dynamic path operand is owned by the same indexed
  output scope/list as the update branch output. A row field that is TEXT in
  some other list is no longer enough to pass `verify_plan`.
- Added live mixed-row-shape negative fixtures for indexed row-field dynamic
  paths. They target row key `2` where the path field is absent and require the
  static scheduler to reject the branch with
  `is not in the static schedule symbol table`.
- Added fabricated verifier-only `fabricated-row-field-path-foreign-list`
  cases for both `File/read_bytes` and `File/write_bytes`. These mutate a valid
  plan so the output is indexed in one synthetic scope while the TEXT path field
  exists only in a different synthetic list/scope.
- Report schema mirrors the new live and fabricated cases, including the
  recomputed fabricated plan hashes, so reports cannot omit or forge this
  evidence.

Measured evidence:

| Report | Live negatives | Fabricated negatives | New mixed-row-shape case | New foreign-list case |
| --- | ---: | ---: | --- | --- |
| `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json` | `16` | `15` | `rejected=true`, `expected_error_contains=is not in the static schedule symbol table` | `rejected=true`, failed checks include `file-read-bytes-ops-well-formed` |
| `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json` | `17` | `15` | `rejected=true`, `expected_error_contains=is not in the static schedule symbol table` | `rejected=true`, failed checks include `file-write-bytes-ops-well-formed` |

Aggregate evidence:

- `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `aggregate_checks_pass=true`
  - `required_report_count=47`
  - `checked_report_count=47`
  - `proof_report_count=40`
  - `diagnostic_report_count=7`
  - `plan_executor_report_count=33`
  - `no_fallback_plan_executor_count=33`

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `timeout 180s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Final formatting check after rustfmt. |
| `timeout 300s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo check -p boon_plan -p xtask -p boon_report_schema --quiet` | Pass | Focused compile for verifier, xtask, and schema edits; existing native GPU dead-code warnings only. |
| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo build -p xtask --quiet` | Pass | Rebuilt current `xtask` before report generation; existing native GPU dead-code warnings only. |
| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 ./target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-file-read-plan --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json` | Pass | Read gate generated 16 live negatives and 15 fabricated plan negatives. |
| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 ./target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-file-write-plan --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json` | Pass | Write gate generated 17 live negatives and 15 fabricated plan negatives. |
| `timeout 120s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 ./target/debug/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json` | Pass | Schema accepted both updated child reports and recomputed fabricated plan hashes. |
| `timeout 1800s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 bash -lc '<refresh stale child reports from command_argv>'` | Interrupted, then Pass on rerun | First quiet refresh was interrupted while `verify-bytes-machine-plan-adversarial` used one CPU core for several minutes. A rerun skipped fresh reports, refreshed the remaining stale reports, and exited `0`. |
| `timeout 300s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 ./target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Pass | Aggregate passed after all stale child reports were refreshed. |

Independent review:

- Subagent `019ee90e-4455-7e61-966f-f422e7e0977f`
  (`indexed row-field ownership auditor`) recommended this slice and identified
  the concrete verifier gap: `File/read_bytes` / `File/write_bytes` accepted a
  `ValueRef::Field(field_id)` path if the field was TEXT somewhere in initial
  rows, without proving it belonged to the indexed output scope/list.

Open findings:

- Same-event `File/read_bytes` / `File/write_bytes` scheduling remains an
  engine/runtime question and is intentionally not encoded as a negative in
  this slice.
- Missing private BYTES state remains best handled as report/schema tamper or
  a direct runtime invariant test, not as a valid Boon source fixture.
- This hardening does not complete broad Phase 9 performance coverage, full
  workspace tests, or Phase 10 default-switch readiness.

## Phase 9 TodoMVC Release Benchmark Reproduction Gate

Status: partial Phase 9 verification/performance evidence; complete for the
aggregate-required TodoMVC repeated release benchmark integrity gate.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5`

Files changed:

- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

  `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-release-benchmark-repeat.json`
- added/refreshed
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-release-benchmark-reproduction.json`
- refreshed stale aggregate children:
  `bytes-negative.json`, `bytes-machine-plan-adversarial.json`,
  `bytes-file-read-plan.json`, `bytes-file-write-plan.json`,
  `bytes-storage-profile.json`, `bytes-byte-bank-layout.json`,
  `build-bytes-boundary.json`, and the nested large source-payload route
  report.
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

What changed:

- Added xtask command `verify-bytes-release-benchmark-reproduction`.
- Added aggregate child
  `todomvc-release-benchmark-reproduction` at
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-release-benchmark-reproduction.json`.
- Added report-schema validation for the reproduction proof. A passing report
  must prove:
  - at least two release benchmark reports;
  - schema-valid child benchmark reports;
  - fresh linked benchmark artifacts;
  - consistent benchmark identity across reports;
  - release build profile;
  - no speedup claim;
  - repeated metric max/min ratios within the current loose policy.
- Added the same child to the schema-side exact aggregate child list.
- Left speedup claims disabled. The new gate proves repeated-measurement
  integrity, not a user-facing performance improvement.

- Subagent `019ee8d3-0dfb-7742-ae01-a9969638f682`
  (`Raman the 3rd`) audited the ledger and code and recommended this as the
  next dependency-ready non-0804 Phase 9 slice.
- The reviewer found the reproduction command/aggregate registration path was
  the right next target and warned that old aggregate artifacts were stale after
  xtask rebuilds.
- The same review recommended indexed dynamic-path negative expansion and a
  Phase 10 readiness preflight as the next candidates after this slice.

Open findings:

- This closes the TodoMVC repeated release benchmark integrity gate only. The
  ratio policy is intentionally loose (`<= 5.0`) and does not justify speedup
  claims.
- The Phase 0 baseline speed report used for comparison remains an old baseline
  artifact and is stale by current binary hash; benchmark reports bind it as a
  baseline artifact, not as current execution evidence.
- Cells release benchmark evidence remains open/postponed outside this slice.
- Full BYTES/MachinePlan performance coverage, stricter repeated benchmark
  reproduction policy, full workspace tests, final adversarial audit breadth,
  and Phase 10 default switch readiness remain open.

indexed row-field dynamic-path `File/read_bytes` / `File/write_bytes`
negative matrix.

  `build-bytes-boundary.json`, and the nested large source-payload route

What changed:

- Expanded live indexed row-field dynamic-path `File/read_bytes` negative
  coverage with:
  - `runtime-indexed-row-field-missing-file`
  - `runtime-indexed-row-field-fixed-output-length-mismatch`
  - `typecheck-indexed-row-field-non-text-path`
  - `runtime-indexed-row-field-missing-target-row`

  coverage with:
  - `runtime-indexed-row-field-missing-parent`
  - `typecheck-indexed-row-field-non-text-path`
  - `runtime-indexed-row-field-missing-target-row`
  - `typecheck-indexed-row-field-unknown-path-field`
- Mirrored these expected case IDs, stages, categories, target kinds, and
  expected error substrings in `boon_report_schema`, so forged reports cannot
  omit the new cases.
- Kept same-event receive/read/write ordering out of this negative matrix. The
  subagent warned that same-event ordering may expose an engine scheduling
  limitation that should be fixed rather than frozen as a negative.

| read | `runtime-indexed-row-field-fixed-output-length-mismatch` | plan executor | `fixed length 1 does not match produced length 4` |
| read | `typecheck-indexed-row-field-non-text-path` | typecheck | `File/read_bytes` |
| read | `runtime-indexed-row-field-missing-target-row` | plan executor | `target key/generation 999/1 not found` |
| read | `typecheck-indexed-row-field-unknown-path-field` | typecheck | `missing_path` |
| write | `runtime-indexed-row-field-missing-parent` | plan executor | `cannot write missing-parent/out.bin` |
| write | `typecheck-indexed-row-field-non-text-path` | typecheck | `File/write_bytes` |
| write | `runtime-indexed-row-field-missing-target-row` | plan executor | `target key/generation 999/1 not found` |
| write | `typecheck-indexed-row-field-unknown-path-field` | typecheck | `missing_path` |

Independent review:

- Subagent `019ee8ef-7498-77f2-8753-ded793c98226`
  (`Kierkegaard the 3rd`) audited current live/fabricated negative coverage and
  confirmed the first added cases were already present in the working tree.
- The reviewer recommended adding missing target row and unknown row path field
  live cases next, which this slice implemented for both read and write.
- The reviewer also warned not to freeze same-event ordering as a negative if
  it exposes an engine scheduling limitation.

Open findings:

- Mixed row-shape and foreign-list row-field references remain open.
- Missing private BYTES state is more likely a report/schema tamper case than a
  valid source fixture, because valid Boon source creates the private BYTES
  sidecar automatically.
- Same-event receive/read/write scheduling remains open as an engine/runtime
  behavior question, not a negative fixture in this slice.
- This remains focused negative-matrix evidence. It does not satisfy broad
  BYTES/MachinePlan performance coverage, Cells release benchmark evidence,
  full workspace tests, or Phase 10 default switch readiness.

## TASK-0804A Cells Release Benchmark Debugging Loop

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T08:13:06+02:00`

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Status:

- `TASK-0804A` remains unfinished and is postponed after three bounded
  debugging loops.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark.json` is still missing
  because `cargo xtask bench-example cells ...` still exits before writing the
  wrapper report when speed budgets fail.
- The current speed-layer report is
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark-speed.json`.

Independent review:

- Subagent `019ee8be-45b0-7ea0-8f8a-a013941f9d5a`
  (`Lagrange the 3rd`) performed a read-only audit with `rg`, `jq`, and `sed`.
- The reviewer independently identified the hot path as
  `store.selected_input`, defined by
  `List/find(cells, field: address, value: selected_address)`, flowing through
  `apply_source_actions` ->
  `flush_root_derived_source_action_changes` ->
  `materialize_root_derived_field_commits_for_changed_reads` ->
  `materialize_root_list_view_field_with_cache_state`.
- The reviewer confirmed the slow samples are single-row selected-input
  materializations, not whole-sheet scans, and recommended a direct `List/find`
  root-list path plus later same-event materialization/followup coalescing.

Debugging loops completed:

1. Scoped bool-context collection by source route.
   - Cause addressed: `bool_context_ms` was previously a dominant cost because
     every source event built broad generic bool context.
   - Result: `bool_context_ms` dropped to roughly microsecond scale in later
     Cells benchmark runs.
   - Not enough: the benchmark remained dominated by source-action root
     materialization/flush.
2. Field-scoped text/numeric lookup-index invalidation.
   - Cause addressed: single text field writes invalidated all lookup indexes
     for a list, so formula/value edits could evict the unrelated
     `cells.address` lookup index needed by selection.
   - Runtime change: ordinary `set_textlike` / scalar text-enum-json writes now
     invalidate lookup indexes only for the changed field and skip invalidation
     when the value is unchanged.
   - Test added:
     `list_index_text_lookup_survives_unrelated_text_field_mutation`.
   - Result: selected-input materialization improved but still failed budgets.
3. Direct compiled `List/find` root-list materialization path.
   - Cause addressed: the selected-input root list view was paying generic
     expression evaluation before discovering it was a single-row indexed
     `List/find` projection.
   - Runtime change:
     `try_materialize_root_list_find_projection_field` uses
     `ListProjectionPlan` directly for `Find` projections, resolves the
     selector root, performs the existing indexed lookup, materializes the
     selected row snapshot, records root/list reads, and then uses the same
     diff/replace/rebind path as the generic list-view materializer.
   - Result: some selected-input refreshes now run near `0.03 ms`, but cold or
     invalidated selected-input refreshes still spike high enough to fail the
     Cells release benchmark.

Latest benchmark evidence after loop 3:

| Metric | Current measured value |
| --- | --- |
| `bool_context_ms` | p50 `0.000493`, p95 `0.001021`, max `0.007619` |
| `source_action_root_materialization_ms` | p50 `0.064335`, p95 `7.726983`, max `8.497491` |
| `source_action_root_flush_ms` | p50 `0.0`, p95 `8.063869`, max `8.785625` |
| `source_action_eval_ms` | p50 `0.442184`, p95 `7.844088`, max `9.132819` |
| Slowest current step | `edit-a0-literal`, latency `9.155074 ms` |
| Worst current selected-input sample | `8.465557 ms`, `eval_ms=7.453642`, `changed_read_count=14`, `dirty_root_read_count=2` |

Observed improvement:

- Before these loops, the latest failing Cells benchmark showed
  `input_to_idle` p50 `8.655364`, p95 `14.832171`, max `16.891415`.
- After loop 3, the max observed step dropped to `9.155074 ms` in the current
  speed report, and several repeated selected-input materializations are now
  around `0.02-0.04 ms`.
- This is real movement, but not a pass: the budget still requires p95 `<= 4 ms`
  and max `<= 8 ms`.

Remaining suspected cause:

- The remaining high samples are no longer broad bool context and no longer
  whole-list scans. They are still selected-input source-action flushes where
  the root-list materialization has only one row but spends several ms in the
  direct `List/find`/selector/currentness path.
- The next systematic fix should not be another blind micro-change. It should
  add sub-timers inside the direct path for selector currentness,
  `cached_find_list_indices_by_textlike_indexed`, row snapshotting, diff, and
  replace/rebind, then decide whether the real problem is index rebuild,
  deferred root-currentness, duplicate same-event materialization, or storage
  snapshot/diff churn.
- The subagent's second recommendation remains open: coalesce same-event
  selected root materializations/followups so one source event cannot
  rematerialize `store.selected_input` multiple times when the later dirty set
  is already covered.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `timeout 180s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Formatting gate before and after direct path edits. |
| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo check -p boon_runtime --quiet` | Pass | Focused runtime compile after index invalidation and direct `List/find` path. |
| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime list_index_text_lookup_survives_unrelated_text_field_mutation -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | Proves unrelated text field mutation does not evict an existing address lookup index. |
| `timeout 300s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime cells_selected_input_list_find_materializes_single_row_storage -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | Existing selected-input storage behavior still passes. |
| `timeout 480s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo xtask bench-example cells --iterations 20 --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark.json --speed-report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark-speed.json` | Fail | Still fails `latency_max_budget` and `latency_p95_budget`; no wrapper report is written. |

Decision:

- Stop working on `TASK-0804A` for now and move to other unfinished Phase 9 work.
- Do not mark Phase 9 complete.
- Do not add `cells-release-benchmark.json` to the aggregate yet, because no
  honest passing wrapper exists.

## TASK-0804A Direct `List/find` Timing Follow-up

Recorded at: `2026-06-21T14:17:35+02:00`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Status:

- `TASK-0804A` remains unfinished and postponed.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark.json` is still absent
  because the benchmark wrapper correctly refuses to write a passing release
  report while speed budgets fail.
- The current diagnostic speed report is
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark-speed.json`.

Independent review:

- Subagent `019ee9fd-c4da-7e01-928b-c5bc9279c3b9`
  (`Halley the 4th`) performed a read-only audit. The reviewer confirmed the
  current command shape, that TodoMVC has release/reproduction evidence while
  Cells does not, and that the smallest honest next slice was engine-first
  direct-path timing before adding any Cells aggregate child.

What the new measurements found:

- The earlier suspicion that `store.selected_input` was still slow inside
  row lookup was too coarse.
- Direct-path sub-timers showed the first post-instrumentation culprit was a
  one-shot runtime text lookup cache insert:
  - `lookup_index_rebuilt=false`, so `cells.address` was not being rebuilt.
  - `lookup_storage_ms` was only about `0.002-0.003 ms`.
  - `lookup_cache_insert_ms` reached about `6.77 ms` even with
    `lookup_cache_entry_count=0` and `lookup_candidate_count=1`.
- Runtime change: the direct root `List/find` materialization path now bypasses
  the runtime lookup cache and reads the existing storage text index directly.
  That cache is still used by the generic expression paths; the direct path had
  zero cache hits in the measured samples and paid insertion cost for a
  one-shot value.
- After this fix, direct lookup is no longer the hot path:
  - `max_indexed_lookup_ms=0.004071`
  - `max_lookup_storage_ms=0.004014`
  - `max_lookup_cache_insert_ms=0.0`

Remaining measured cause:

- Cells still fails the speed budget because the slow selected-input samples
  moved to root read dependency replacement:
  - `input_to_idle` p50 `6.705710`, p95 `9.666593`, p99 `9.788852`, max
    `9.788852`
  - budget requires p95 `<= 4.0 ms` and max `<= 8.0 ms`
  - worst direct selected-input samples now have `eval_ms` around `0.004-0.008`
    but `root_read_commit_ms` up to `7.619788`
  - representative worst sample:
    `elapsed_ms=8.965949`, `changed_read_count=14`,
    `indexed_lookup_ms=0.002110`, `root_read_commit_ms=7.619788`,
    `diff_ms=0.669472`, `replace_ms=0.376812`
- The next systematic fix should target
  `generic_derived_state.replace_root_reads` / root read dependency storage for
  small direct-list projections. Likely options are avoiding replacement when
  the read set is unchanged, using a cheaper small-set representation for root
  reads, or coalescing same-event selected-root materializations before read
  dependency replacement.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `timeout 180s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Passed after applying `cargo fmt --all` once for line wrapping. |
| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo check -p boon_runtime --quiet` | Pass | Focused compile for runtime profiling and cache-bypass edits. |
| `timeout 300s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime cells_selected_input_list_find_materializes_single_row_storage -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | Selected-input storage behavior remains correct after direct cache bypass. |
| `timeout 480s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo xtask bench-example cells --iterations 20 --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark.json --speed-report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark-speed.json` | Fail | Still fails `latency_max_budget` and `latency_p95_budget`; no wrapper report is written. |

Decision:

- Keep the cache-bypass fix and the direct-path timing fields because they
  found and removed a real measured cost.
- Do not continue blind TASK-0804A loops in this slice. The task remains
  postponed with a narrower next target: root-read dependency replacement for
  direct selected-list projections.
- Do not add Cells release benchmark or reproduction reports to the aggregate
  until an honest passing wrapper exists.

## TASK-0804A Scheduler/Profile Cost Follow-up

Recorded at: `2026-06-22T03:25:50+02:00`

Base/current commit: `6494b54e488dc63cfb39d0e1d50830f1283b80dc`

Files changed in this slice:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`

Status:

- `TASK-0804A` remains unfinished.
- `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark.json` is still missing
  because the benchmark wrapper correctly refuses to write a passing report
  while the Cells release speed budget fails.
- The current diagnostic report is
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark-speed.json`.

Independent review:

- Subagent `019eecdc-a75a-74b1-8d91-66b4f90c3a4b`
  (`Jason the 4th`) performed a read-only audit. The reviewer confirmed that
  the previous report was stale relative to the dirty scheduler buckets and
  identified the likely unbucketed work inside
  `materialize_root_derived_field_commits_for_changed_reads` as:
  `remove_structured_child_ready_roots`, `record_root_materialization_sample`,
  and `prune_structured_child_dirty_roots_for_changed_reads`.
- The same review also warned that if those buckets did not absorb the time,
  the fixed `6-8 ms` shape could be wall-clock descheduling rather than normal
  Rust work.

What the refreshed measurements proved:

- After adding the scheduler buckets, the earlier large
  `source_action_root_dirty_scheduler_unattributed_ms` mostly disappeared.
- `source_action_root_dirty_ready_refresh_ms` and
  `source_action_root_prune_structured_ms` stayed near microsecond scale.
- The selection-step root flush spikes moved to
  `source_action_root_record_sample_ms`, while actual root-list
  materialization stayed tiny.
- Representative pre-fix refreshed samples:
  - `select-a3-for-literal-entry`: latency `7.944764 ms`,
    `root_flush=7.865871`, `root_dirty_scheduler=7.697502`,
    `record_sample=7.668707`, `root_materialization=0.148521`,
    `eval=0.015575`.
  - `edit-a0-literal`: latency `6.418822 ms`, `root_flush=5.645331`,
    `root_dirty_scheduler=5.608430`, `record_sample=5.564283`,
    `root_materialization=0.069671`, `eval=0.557781`.
  - Formula/value edits remained dominated by indexed row recomputation, e.g.
    `type-a3-literal-20`: latency `9.908217 ms`, `eval=9.688423`,
    `indexed_followup_row_recompute=9.646797`.

Runtime changes:

- Added `source_action_root_dirty_ready_refresh_ms`,
  `source_action_root_record_sample_ms`, and
  `source_action_root_prune_structured_ms` to `LiveRuntimeStepProfile` so the
  root dirty scheduler timing is no longer a single large unattributed bucket.
- Added non-test diagnostic gates:
  - `BOON_PROFILE_ROOT_MATERIALIZATION_SAMPLES=1` keeps full
    root-materialization samples.
  - `BOON_PROFILE_ROOT_MATERIALIZATION_DETAILS=1` keeps detailed list-view
    aggregates under root-materialization by-path stats.
- Tests still keep those detailed profiles enabled by default, preserving the
  existing unit-test diagnostics.

Results after the first diagnostic gate:

- Full sample retention was disabled in the release benchmark.
- `latency_max_budget` passed, but `latency_p95_budget` still failed:
  - p50 `3.898245 ms`
  - p95 `7.357230 ms`
  - p99/max `7.396376 ms`
  - max budget `<= 8 ms` passed
  - p95 budget `<= 4 ms` failed
- Retained root-materialization samples were `0`, but selection steps still
  showed large `source_action_root_record_sample_ms`, proving the retained
  sample vector itself was not the complete cause.

Results after the second diagnostic gate:

- Detailed list-view by-path aggregation was also disabled in the release
  benchmark.
- The benchmark remained unstable/failing:
  - p50 `2.830359 ms`
  - p95 `7.071146 ms`
  - p99/max `9.382550 ms`
  - max budget `<= 8 ms` failed
  - p95 budget `<= 4 ms` failed
- The report confirms `root_materialization_stats.samples=0` and by-path stats
  contain only lightweight counters for the slow selection samples, but
  `source_action_root_record_sample_ms` still caught wall-clock stalls:
  - `select-b0-shows-formula-in-bar`: latency `9.382550 ms`,
    `root_flush=8.353696`, `root_dirty_scheduler=8.047402`,
    `record_sample=8.020761`, `root_materialization=0.286612`.
  - `select-a3-for-literal-entry`: latency `6.242231 ms`,
    `root_flush=5.699045`, `root_dirty_scheduler=5.646100`,
    `record_sample=5.621809`, `root_materialization=0.036942`.
  - `select-c0-for-formula-update`: latency `5.831525 ms`,
    `root_flush=5.585813`, `root_dirty_scheduler=5.529483`,
    `record_sample=5.038162`, `root_materialization=0.040688`.

Current conclusion:

- One real cause was measurement/report overhead: full root-materialization
  samples and list-view aggregate details were being maintained on the measured
  release hot path even though the speed budget does not need them. That is now
  controlled by explicit diagnostic environment variables.
- That was not enough to finish `TASK-0804A`. The remaining budget failures
  have two distinct shapes:
  - selection steps still show wall-clock stalls around the
    root-materialization record/drop boundary even after retained samples and
    detailed by-path aggregates are disabled;
  - formula/value edit steps are dominated by
    `source_action_indexed_followup_row_recompute_ms`, indicating the Cells
    row recompute path still evaluates too much or repeats formula work.
- The next useful TASK-0804A continuation should add a wall-time vs thread-CPU
  timer around `record_root_materialization_sample` and the caller-side
  `result.profile` drop boundary, then separately target formula/value
  recomputation with a real dependency-frontier or function/formula cache
  design. Continuing with blind micro-edits is not justified.

Commands run:

| Command | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `timeout 900s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo build --release -p xtask --quiet` | Pass | Built the dirty scheduler-bucket instrumentation before the first refreshed benchmark; existing native GPU dead-code warnings were emitted. |
| `timeout 1200s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/release/xtask bench-example cells --iterations 20 --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark.json --speed-report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark-speed.json` | Fail | Refreshed stale report; failed `latency_max_budget` and `latency_p95_budget`, but identified `record_sample` and indexed row recompute as the two hot shapes. |
| `timeout 180s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo fmt --all -- --check` | Pass | Passed before the diagnostic gates; later failed only on wrapping after the second gate and was corrected with `cargo fmt --all`. |
| `timeout 300s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo check -p boon_runtime --quiet` | Pass | Passed after each diagnostic gate. |
| `timeout 360s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo test -p boon_runtime cells_selected_input_list_find_materializes_single_row_storage -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` | Pass | Passed after each diagnostic gate; detailed test diagnostics remain enabled under `cfg(test)`. |
| `timeout 900s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo build --release -p xtask --quiet` | Pass | Rebuilt after the first diagnostic gate; existing native GPU dead-code warnings were emitted. |
| `timeout 1200s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/release/xtask bench-example cells --iterations 20 --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark.json --speed-report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark-speed.json` | Fail | After disabling full sample retention, max passed at `7.396376 ms`, but p95 still failed at `7.357230 ms`; wrapper report remained missing. |
| `timeout 180s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo fmt --all` | Pass | Applied rustfmt wrapping after the second diagnostic gate. |
| `timeout 900s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo build --release -p xtask --quiet` | Pass | Rebuilt after disabling detailed list-view by-path aggregation; existing native GPU dead-code warnings were emitted. |
| `timeout 1200s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/release/xtask bench-example cells --iterations 20 --report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark.json --speed-report target/reports/bytes-plan/cells-release-benchmark-speed.json` | Fail | After disabling detailed list-view by-path aggregation, p95 `7.071146 ms` and max `9.382550 ms` still failed; wrapper report remained missing. |

Decision:

- Keep the diagnostic gates and scheduler buckets because they make the report
  overhead explicit and avoid retaining heavy diagnostics by default in
  non-test release measurements.
- Do not mark `TASK-0804A` complete.
- Do not add Cells release benchmark evidence to the aggregate.
- Move to other unfinished goal work after recording this evidence unless a
  future focused TASK-0804A turn is explicitly requested.

## Phase 9 Focused Aggregate Adversarial Hardening

Status: partial Phase 9 adversarial verification evidence; complete for the
covered focused tamper matrix.

What changed:

- Runtime capacity reports now include BYTES capacity metadata:
  `all_bytes_bounded` plus per-state `bytes` entries with `fixed_len`,
  `effective_capacity`, capacity source, growth policy, and overflow behavior.
- `boon_cli explain-hardware` now emits the same BYTES capacity metadata and
  includes the required `measurement_mode=diagnostic` field.
- Report schema now rejects `hardware_bounded` runtime-profile reports when
  `capacities.all_bytes_bounded=false`.
- `verify-bytes-negative` grew from 41 to 49 schema-required negative cases.
  The new required cases are:
  - `aggregate-missing-child-report-rejected`
  - `aggregate-stale-child-artifact-hash-rejected`

  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json` aggregate child, run
  the real `verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing` gate, assert
  rejection, and restore the original child report.
- The fake benchmark case mutates
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/todomvc-release-benchmark.json` by injecting an
  unlinked fake `stress_profiles` payload and proves schema rejection against
  the linked speed report copy/hash contract.

## Phase 7/9 Indexed Dynamic-Path Negative Hardening

Status: partial, focused hardening complete for the covered subset.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed child reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`

What changed:

- `boon_plan` now counts indexed row-field dynamic-path `File/read_bytes` as
  CPU-supported when it has an indexed output BYTES state, no source payload
  dependency, no update constant, and a supported row-field TEXT path operand.
- `verify-bytes-file-read-plan` now includes the live negative case
  `runtime-indexed-row-field-parent-path`, where the indexed row path resolves
  to `../small.bin` and is rejected by the host-boundary path policy.
- `verify-bytes-file-write-plan` now includes the live negative case
  `runtime-indexed-row-field-parent-path`, where the indexed row path resolves
  to `../indexed.bin` and is rejected by the same path policy.
- Both file-read and file-write fabricated verifier matrices now include:
  - `fabricated-row-field-path-op-not-indexed`
  - `fabricated-row-field-path-input-missing`
- Report schema replay now mirrors indexed `Bytes/length` and `Bytes/get`
  `bytes_access` metadata for fixed indexed byte banks. This fixed a real
  schema replay drift where the runtime correctly reported
  `indexed_fixed_byte_bank`, but schema reconstruction hardcoded
  `bytes_access=null` for indexed update rows.

| `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json` | `live_negative_case_count=12`, `fabricated_plan_negative_case_count=14`; `runtime-indexed-row-field-parent-path` rejected with `may not contain parent-directory segments`; fabricated row-field cases rejected by `file-write-bytes-ops-well-formed` and capability checks. |
| `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | `status=pass`, `aggregate_checks_pass=true`, `required_report_count=45`, `checked_report_count=45`, `proof_report_count=38`, `diagnostic_report_count=7`, `plan_executor_report_count=33`, `no_fallback_plan_executor_count=33`, `blocker_count=0`, `binary_hash=23cb8005e22c835abf448c4293e2aff18c53ebd468dba6618b99839ff209e481`. |

## Phase 7/9 Indexed Row-Field Dynamic-Path File/write_bytes

Status: partial Phase 7 storage/runtime evidence and Phase 9 verification
evidence; complete for indexed row-field dynamic-path `File/write_bytes` where
the path is a typed TEXT row field.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-indexed-dynamic-file-write-path-review.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
- refreshed the aggregate report at
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

What changed:

- The generated indexed storage-profile fixture now gives each row a
  `path_state: TEXT { outputs/indexed.bin }` field and writes with
  `payload |> File/write_bytes(path: path_state)`.
- The MachinePlan for that write lowers to ordered operands
  `[State(BYTES payload), Field(TEXT path_state)]`, not a static path
  constant and not a Boon-level `HOLD` workaround.
- `boon_plan` now accepts indexed `FileWriteBytes` path operands that are typed
  row fields when the row initial field metadata proves they are TEXT.
- The indexed PlanExecutor now resolves row-field path operands from the
  currently targeted row, uses the same sandboxed host write boundary as
  static and root dynamic paths, and records:
  - `bytes_access.path_source=row_field`
  - `update_constant_value.path_source=row_field`
  - `update_constant_value.path_field=row.path_state`
  - `update_constant_id=null`
- The proof keeps the existing indexed fixed byte-bank borrowed access evidence:
  `access_source=indexed_fixed_byte_bank`, `byte_bank_used=true`,
  `cow_kind=borrowed`, and zero byte-copy counters.
- Report schema now requires `dynamic_path_matches=true` only for the indexed
  row-field dynamic-path case, while preserving the older static and root
  dynamic file-write evidence shapes.

Evidence from `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`:

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Report status | `pass` |
| Case | `indexed-file-write-source-payload-no-copy` |
| Case status | `pass` |
| `borrowed_read_evidence.dynamic_path_matches` | `true` |
| `borrowed_read_evidence.borrowed_file_write` | `true` |
| `borrowed_read_evidence.artifact_matches` | `true` |
| `borrowed_read_evidence.host_effect_matches` | `true` |
| `borrowed_read_evidence.step_zero_byte_counters` | `true` |
| Write value | `outputs/indexed.bin` |
| `update_constant_id` | `null` |
| `update_constant_value.path_source` | `row_field` |
| `update_constant_value.path_field` | `row.path_state` |

  dynamic path negatives remain open.
- Indexed dynamic-path `File/read_bytes` is still open. The reviewer identified
  it as a larger follow-up because indexed `FileReadBytes` is not yet counted
  as CPU-supported and has no indexed executor arm.
- The focused aggregate is current and passing again for the checked BYTES,
  TodoMVC, and Cells report set, but it is still not a final Phase 9 exit
  because broad performance evidence and adversarial/tamper coverage remain
  incomplete.

  workspace tests, broad performance evidence, or final adversarial audit.

## Phase 7/9 Indexed Row-Field Dynamic-Path File/read_bytes

Status: partial Phase 7 storage/runtime evidence and Phase 9 verification
evidence; complete for indexed row-field dynamic-path `File/read_bytes` where
the path is a typed TEXT row field and the output is a fixed indexed BYTES
state.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

What changed:

- `boon_plan` now accepts indexed `FileReadBytes` path operands that are typed
  row fields when initial row field metadata proves the field is TEXT.
- The indexed PlanExecutor now resolves `File/read_bytes` row-field paths from
  the current row, uses the same sandboxed host read boundary as root dynamic
  paths, and rejects row-field path operands in root execution.
- Indexed `Bytes/length` and `Bytes/get` now report byte-bank access evidence
  from `indexed_row_state_bytes_view`, including `access_source`,
  `cow_kind`, `byte_bank_declared`, and `byte_bank_used`.
- `verify-bytes-storage-profile` now generates
  `bytes_indexed_file_read_plan_ops.bn` / `.scn` plus a real host input file
  under `target/generated/bytes-storage-profile-file-write-fixtures/inputs`.
  The scenario reads through `path_state: row.path_state`, stores the result in
  `row.file_bytes`, then proves a later `Bytes/get` reads from the indexed
  fixed byte bank.
- Report schema now treats this as an explicit host-boundary case where legacy
  comparison is disabled, and verifies the row-field path metadata plus the
  later borrowed indexed byte-bank read.

## Phase 7/9 BYTES OOB No-Panic Negative Hardening

Status: partial Phase 7 runtime-safety and Phase 9 verification evidence;
complete for the focused static `Bytes/slice`, runtime `Bytes/get`, and static
`Bytes/set` out-of-bounds negative cases.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-oob-no-panic-review.json`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-dynamic-file-path-review.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json`
- refreshed stale `xtask` child reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

What changed:

- Added focused `verify-bytes-negative` plan cases:
  - `plan-bytes-get-out-of-bounds`
  - `plan-bytes-set-out-of-bounds`
- Wrapped negative PlanExecutor execution in
  `std::panic::catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(...))`.
- Negative plan cases now record:
  - `failure_kind="structured_error"` when the runtime/compiler rejects the
    case through ordinary error propagation.
  - `failure_kind="panic"` and `panic_free=false` if the PlanExecutor panics.
  - `failure_kind="accepted"` if a negative fixture is incorrectly accepted.
- Report-schema validation now requires `panic_free=true` and
  `failure_kind="structured_error"` for:
  - `plan-static-out-of-bounds-slice`
  - `plan-bytes-get-out-of-bounds`
  - `plan-bytes-set-out-of-bounds`

  the smallest coherent next safety slice and explicitly warned not to treat it
  as zero-allocation proof.

  (`plan/runtime parity auditor`) reviewed future dynamic host-boundary work

Open findings:

- This proof is focused: it proves structured, panic-free rejection for three
  OOB cases in the negative gate. It does not prove every BYTES builtin and
  every indexed/root storage path is panic-free.
- Static `Bytes/slice` and static `Bytes/set` OOB are rejected by MachinePlan
  verification; runtime `Bytes/get` OOB is rejected by PlanExecutor with a
  structured runtime error.
- Zero-allocation fixed-byte warm ticks remain open. The OOB hardening only
  classifies rejection behavior; it is not heap-allocation evidence.
- Dynamic root-scalar path `File/write_bytes` and `File/read_bytes` are no
  longer deferred. Indexed dynamic file paths and broader malformed path
  matrices remain open and are recorded in the dynamic file-path reviewer
  reports.

## Phase 7/9 Root Dynamic-Path File/write_bytes

Status: partial Phase 7 host-boundary runtime/storage and Phase 9 verification
evidence; complete for root-scalar dynamic-path `File/write_bytes` where the
path is a typed TEXT state.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-dynamic-file-write-path-review.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json`
- refreshed stale `xtask` child reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

What changed:

- Added shared `boon_ir::FileBytesPath` support with:
  - `StaticText(String)`
  - `StatePath(String)`
- Static literal paths still use the existing source-time safe-relative policy:
  non-empty, not absolute, and no parent-directory segments.
- Dynamic path arguments such as
  `payload |> File/write_bytes(path: store.path_state)` are now canonicalized
  as typed state references instead of becoming an unsupported expression.
- MachinePlan lowering now emits:
  - `[State(bytes), Constant(path)]` for static paths.
  - `[State(bytes), State(path_text)]` for root dynamic paths.
- MachinePlan verification accepts dynamic path operands only when the second
  operand is a declared scalar TEXT state input. Indexed dynamic paths are
  still rejected because row-local dynamic path semantics are not designed yet.
- Root PlanExecutor resolves `State(path_text)` from `root_state`, reports
  `bytes_access.path_source="state"`, and uses the same
  `write_plan_host_file_bytes` sandbox policy as static paths.
- The old live negative that proved dynamic paths unsupported was removed and
  replaced with:
  - a positive dynamic path proof in `verify-bytes-file-write-plan`.
  - a runtime negative `runtime-dynamic-parent-path` proving
    `TEXT { ../dynamic.bin }` in a path state is rejected by the host-boundary
    sandbox.
- Report schema now requires `dynamic_path_file_write_summary` and validates
  it by recompiling the dynamic fixture source into a source-derived
  MachinePlan. It checks `[State(BYTES), State(TEXT)]`, plan hash, artifact
  digest/length, state-sourced path evidence, zero fallback counters, and no
  inline byte leakage.

  dynamic paths are rejected, but broader malformed dynamic path matrices remain

## Phase 7/9 Root Dynamic-Path File/read_bytes

Status: partial Phase 7 host-boundary runtime/storage and Phase 9 verification
evidence; complete for root-scalar dynamic-path `File/read_bytes` where the
path is a typed TEXT state.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_ir/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-dynamic-file-read-path-review.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`
- refreshed stale `xtask` child reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

What changed:

- `File/read_bytes` now uses the shared `boon_ir::FileBytesPath` path model:
  - `StaticText(String)`
  - `StatePath(String)`
- Static literal read paths still use the source-time safe-relative policy:
  non-empty, not absolute, and no parent-directory segments.
- Dynamic path arguments such as
  `store.path_state |> File/read_bytes()` are canonicalized as typed scalar
  TEXT state references instead of being rejected or collapsed to strings.
- MachinePlan lowering now emits:
  - `[Constant(path)]` for static read paths.
  - `[State(path_text)]` for root dynamic read paths.
- MachinePlan verification accepts a dynamic read path only when the single
  state input is a declared scalar TEXT slot. The one-operand read shape remains
  separate from the two-operand write shape.
- Root PlanExecutor resolves `State(path_text)` from `root_state`, reports
  `update_constant_value.path_source="state"`, and uses the same
  `read_plan_host_file_bytes` sandbox policy as static paths.
- The old live negative that proved dynamic read paths unsupported was removed
  and replaced with:
  - a positive dynamic path read proof in `verify-bytes-file-read-plan`.
  - a runtime negative `runtime-dynamic-parent-path` proving
    `TEXT { ../small.bin }` in a path state is rejected by the host-boundary
    sandbox.
- The proof also runs later consumers against the read BYTES state:
  `Bytes/length` and `Bytes/get(index: 0)`.
- Report schema now requires `dynamic_path_file_read_summary` and validates it
  by recompiling the dynamic fixture source into a source-derived MachinePlan.
  It checks `[State(TEXT)]`, plan hash, source/scenario hashes, artifact
  digest/length/first byte, state-sourced path evidence, zero fallback
  counters, and no inline byte leakage.

`target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json`:

| --- | --- |
| Report status | `pass` |
| Binary hash | `9079cf5739c1e6bc120352dae5bb85591164d54d7cb1de52ece26021746c78d4` |
| `dynamic_path_file_read_summary.path_operand` | `state` |
| `dynamic_path_file_read_summary.path_state` | `store.path_state` |
| `dynamic_path_file_read_summary.path` | `assets/dynamic-read.bin` |
| `dynamic_path_file_read_summary.byte_len` | `4` |
| `dynamic_path_file_read_summary.first_byte` | `222` |
| `dynamic_path_file_read_summary.sha256` | `5f78c33274e43fa9de5659265c1d917e25c03722dcb0b8d27db8d5feaa813953` |
| Dynamic plan hash | `3824cc7390e3dd4bdec75f471fed64b36e4ad261e053a0eebd5c40a7fbcace3a` |

| Selected steps | `read-file-bytes-dynamic-path`, `inspect-file-bytes-dynamic-path` |
| Dynamic fallback counters | `runtime_ast_eval_count=0`, `executable_string_path_count=0`, `unknown_plan_op_count=0`, `graph_rebuild_count=0`, `graph_clones_per_item=0` |
| `update_constant_value.path_source` | `state` |
| Consumer proof | `store.file_len=4`, `store.first_byte=222` |
| Dynamic negative | `runtime-dynamic-parent-path`, rejected with `host file path ../small.bin may not contain parent-directory segments` |

  (`plan/runtime parity auditor`) identified the static-only read path,
  MachinePlan helper, runtime path operand, schema constant-path requirement,
  and live negative that needed replacement. The implementation followed that
  map without weakening static path policy or fallback counters.
- Reviewer report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-dynamic-file-read-path-review.json`.

Open findings:

- This slice covers root-scalar dynamic-path `File/read_bytes` only.
- Indexed dynamic-path `File/read_bytes` remains unsupported until row-local
  path-state semantics and report evidence are designed.
- Broader malformed dynamic path matrices remain future hardening. This slice
  proves the parent-directory escape rejection for a dynamic TEXT path state.
- The legacy scalar mirror for dynamic `File/read_bytes` remains only a
  compatibility placeholder because accepted host-boundary proof runs are
  PlanExecutor-only and explicitly disable legacy comparison.

Superseded by `Phase 7 Fixed Indexed Byte-Bank Backing`: the current
storage-profile evidence now reports `access_source=indexed_fixed_byte_bank`
and `byte_bank_used=true` for the indexed file-write case. The historical
row-private proof above is retained because it was the previous checkpoint.

Supersession note: the next section closes the specific "final indexed
byte-bank backing remains open" finding for this indexed file-write slice. The
historical evidence above is left intact because it describes the previous
row-private proof before fixed indexed byte-bank backing was implemented.

## Phase 7 Fixed Indexed Byte-Bank Backing

Status: partial Phase 7 storage/runtime evidence; complete for the current
indexed source-payload -> fixed row byte-bank -> indexed `File/write_bytes`
slice.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-fixed-indexed-byte-bank-review.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
- refreshed stale `xtask` child reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

What changed:

- Added row-local fixed indexed byte-bank backing to `PlanListRowState`.
- Populated fixed indexed row banks from initial row values, row defaults,
  append constants, and indexed source-payload/update commits when the
  MachinePlan declares a fixed indexed byte bank for the output state.
- Added fixed-length validation when committing BYTES into an indexed fixed
  bank.
- Made indexed `Bytes/length`, indexed `Bytes/get`, and indexed
  `File/write_bytes` read through the same validated byte view. When a fixed
  row bank exists, the view borrows `indexed_fixed_byte_bank`.
- Repaired `refresh_row_initial_state_fields` so initial-row aliases copy
  hidden BYTES backing, not only public JSON summaries.
- Added `byte_bank_used` to indexed file-write `bytes_access` evidence.
- Updated storage-profile and report-schema checks to require
  `access_source=indexed_fixed_byte_bank`, `byte_bank_declared=true`, and
  `byte_bank_used=true` for the indexed file-write case.

Measured counter and artifact evidence from
`target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`:

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Case status | `pass` |
| Storage-profile case count | `12` |
| `legacy_comparison_enabled` | `false` |
| `bytes_storage_no_copy` | `true` |
| Receive update | indexed `source_payload` to row key `2`, field `payload` |
| Write update | indexed `file_write_bytes` to row key `2`, field `write_status` |
| `bytes_access.access_source` | `indexed_fixed_byte_bank` |
| `bytes_access.byte_bank_declared` | `true` |
| `bytes_access.byte_bank_used` | `true` |
| `bytes_access.cow_kind` | `borrowed` |
| `bytes_access.host_boundary` | `file_write_bytes` |
| `bytes_access.byte_len` | `3` |
| Artifact path | `target/generated/bytes-storage-profile-file-write-fixtures/outputs/indexed.bin` |
| Artifact bytes | `01 FE 04` |
| Artifact SHA-256 | `2096dcbc716cabc261901f6c15ce242d3bb589284cf8e97ba196710211d7e99a` |
| `copy_from_slice_bytes` | `0` |
| `vec_clone_bytes` | `0` |
| `vec_alloc_bytes` | `0` |
| `zero_fill_bytes` | `0` |

Important caveat: the receive step copies external event bytes into runtime
storage before the measured write step. This section claims borrowed fixed-bank
access for later indexed reads/writes, not zero-copy external event ingress.

- Subagent `019ee7a9-e724-7b40-aa68-1d8e8de03085`
  (`performance-storage auditor`) confirmed that the plan side already
  declares fixed indexed byte banks, but runtime row BYTES needed real fixed
  bank backing and honest evidence.
- Reviewer report:
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-fixed-indexed-byte-bank-review.json`.

Open findings:

- Row fixed banks are currently keyed by field name to match
  `PlanListRowState`. A later storage cleanup should prefer stable typed IDs
  (`StateId` or field IDs) before more alias-heavy indexed BYTES work.
- This proof covers the indexed source-payload -> fixed row bank -> indexed
  file-write path and the current indexed `Bytes/length` / `Bytes/get` readers.
  It does not complete every fixed indexed byte-bank operation or the full
  Phase 7 zero-allocation fixed-byte warm tick requirement.
- Broad Phase 9 performance evidence, adversarial final audit, full workspace
  tests, and Phase 10 default switch readiness remain open.

## Phase 7 Dynamic Source-Payload File/write_bytes

Status: partial Phase 7 storage/runtime evidence; complete for dynamic root
BYTES state received from a BYTES source payload and later written with
`File/write_bytes`.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`

- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

What changed:

- Added a generated storage-profile fixture under
  `target/generated/bytes-storage-profile-file-write-fixtures/`.
- The fixture runs two ordered steps:
  - `receive-dynamic-bytes`: `store.receive.bytes` receives `01 FE 04` and
    stores it in dynamic root state `store.received`.
  - `write-dynamic-file`: `File/write_bytes` writes `store.received` to
    `outputs/dynamic.bin`.
- Added storage-profile evidence
  `dynamic-file-write-source-payload-no-copy`. The evidence requires the
  source-payload update, `root_bytes_state` / `borrowed` host-boundary access,
  exact artifact digest/bytes, and zero runtime BYTES storage copy counters.
- Added schema validation for this case. The schema requires the legacy
  comparison exception to be explicit for this host-boundary proof and still
  requires legacy comparison for non-file-write storage-profile cases.

## Phase 7 Indexed Row File/write_bytes Borrowed Host Boundary

Status: partial Phase 7 storage/runtime evidence; complete for indexed row
`File/write_bytes` using the current row-private BYTES storage representation
with declared indexed byte-bank metadata.

Base/current commit: `0f891e5f5d49508e3f8618d03913741e58215e11`

Files changed:

- `crates/boon_plan/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/boon_runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/xtask/src/main.rs`
- `crates/boon_report_schema/src/lib.rs`
- `docs/plans/BYTES_AND_MACHINE_PLAN_PROGRESS.md`
- added
  `target/reports/bytes-plan/subagents/phase7-indexed-file-write-review.json`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json`
- refreshed stale `xtask` child reports under `target/reports/bytes-plan/`
- refreshed `target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json`

What changed:

- `boon_plan` now counts indexed `FileWriteBytes` as CPU-supported when the
  indexed update branch has one source, a BYTES state input, a static safe TEXT
  path constant, and a TEXT output state.
- The CPU PlanExecutor now handles generic indexed row `File/write_bytes`
  branches. It resolves the targeted row through the existing source-binding
  route, borrows the row-private BYTES payload, writes it through the same
  sandboxed host boundary as root `File/write_bytes`, and records row-local
  `bytes_access`, `host_effect`, and path constant evidence.
- The generated storage-profile fixture now contains an indexed row case:
  - `receive-indexed-bytes`: writes `01 FE 04` into beta row `payload`.
  - `write-indexed-file`: writes beta row `payload` to `outputs/indexed.bin`.
- Report schema now requires the indexed case, validates row identity, artifact
  bytes, host effect, borrowed access source, declared indexed byte-bank
  metadata, and zero byte-copy counters.

| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 cargo build -p xtask --quiet` | Pass | Rebuilt current `xtask`; existing native GPU dead-code warnings only. |
| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-storage-profile --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json` | Pass | Storage profile passed with 12 cases and the new indexed file-write proof. |
| `timeout 120s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/debug/xtask verify-report-schema target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-storage-profile.json` | Pass | Schema accepted the indexed case and strict artifact/row/access validation. |
| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-machine-plan-all --check-existing --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-machine-plan-all.json` | Fail, then Pass | First run failed because six `xtask` child reports had stale binary hashes after rebuilding `xtask`; passed after refreshing exactly those children. |
| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-negative --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-negative.json` | Pass | Refreshed the negative gate and the nested large source-payload route report. |
| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-file-read-plan --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-read-plan.json` | Pass | Refreshed stale child report. |
| `timeout 240s env CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 nice -n 19 target/debug/xtask verify-bytes-file-write-plan --report target/reports/bytes-plan/bytes-file-write-plan.json` | Pass | Refreshed stale child report. |
